**Cursor**

You already have a flexible PDF export system in place for quotations. Here’s how it works and how you can proceed to make or customize the PDF format for the step2 page:

### How PDF Export Works in Your Project

- The file `src/components/Quotations/QuotationExportPreview.tsx` handles the export logic.
- It uses different PDF templates, such as:
  - `PdfStandardTemplate`
  - `PdfModernTemplate`
  - `PdfMinimalTemplate`
  - `PdfDetailedTemplate`
- The actual PDF is generated using the `html2pdf` library, which converts a React component (the template) into a PDF.
- The template is selected based on the `template` prop and rendered inside a `div` referenced by `pdfContentRef`.
- When the user clicks to download, the content of the selected template is exported as a PDF.

### What You Can Do

1. **To create or customize the PDF format for step2:**
   - Edit or create a new template in `src/components/Quotations/templates/` (e.g., `PdfStep2Template.tsx`).
   - Add your desired layout and data presentation in this template.
   - Update the logic in `QuotationExportPreview.tsx` to include your new template in the `renderTemplate` function.

2. **To use your new format:**
   - Make sure the step2 page passes the correct `template` prop (e.g., `"step2"`) to `QuotationExportPreview`.
   - Add a case for `"step2"` in the `renderTemplate` function to render your new template.

Would you like help creating a new PDF template for step2, or do you want to modify an existing one? If you have a specific layout or fields in mind, please describe them!

### Issues to Address
1. **Quotation From/To:**  
   - The current template does not display "Quotation From" (your company info) and "Quotation To" (customer info) in a standard business format.
2. **Quotation Items:**  
   - The items table is present, but the design is overly colorful and uses gradients.
   - The table should respect column visibility settings (e.g., `show_sqft_in_box`, `show_price_per_box`, etc.), which is already implemented.
3. **Color Scheme:**  
   - The current template uses many gradients and colors. For a standard layout, use a simple, professional color palette (e.g., black, white, one accent color like blue or gray).
4. **General Layout:**  
   - The layout should be clean, with clear sections for "From", "To", "Quotation Details", "Items", and "Totals".

### Plan for the New Standard PDF Template

- **Remove gradients and excessive colors.**
- **Add a "From" section** (your company info, e.g., SmartQuot Solutions).
- **Add a "To" section** (customer info).
- **Keep the items table,** but use a simple table style.
- **Keep column visibility logic** for items.
- **Keep totals and terms,** but style them simply.

```typescriptreact
// ... existing code ...
import React from 'react';
import { Quotation } from '../../../types';
import PDFQuotation from '../../../components/ui/PDFQuotation';

interface PdfStandardTemplateProps {
  quotation: Quotation;
}

const PdfStandardTemplate: React.FC<PdfStandardTemplateProps> = ({ quotation }) => {
  // Fetch company profile from localStorage
  const companyProfile = {
    companyName: typeof window !== 'undefined' ? localStorage.getItem('company_name') || 'Your Company Name' : 'Your Company Name',
    companyAddress: typeof window !== 'undefined' ? localStorage.getItem('company_address') || 'Your Company Address' : 'Your Company Address',
    companyPhone: typeof window !== 'undefined' ? localStorage.getItem('company_phone') || '+91-0000000000' : '+91-0000000000',
    companyEmail: typeof window !== 'undefined' ? localStorage.getItem('company_email') || 'info@yourcompany.com' : 'info@yourcompany.com',
    gstNo: typeof window !== 'undefined' ? localStorage.getItem('gst_no') || '' : '',
  };
  return <PDFQuotation quotation={quotation} companyProfile={companyProfile} />;
};

export default PdfStandardTemplate;
// ... existing code ...
```

```typescriptreact
// ... existing code ...
interface PDFQuotationProps {
  quotation: Quotation;
  companyProfile: {
    companyName: string;
    companyAddress: string;
    companyPhone: string;
    companyEmail: string;
    gstNo?: string;
  };
}

function PDFQuotation({ quotation, companyProfile }: PDFQuotationProps) {
  const formatCurrency = (amount: number) => {
    return new Intl.NumberFormat('en-IN', {
      style: 'currency',
      currency: 'INR',
      minimumFractionDigits: 2
    }).format(amount);
  };

  const formatDate = (dateString: string) => {
    return new Date(dateString).toLocaleDateString('en-IN', {
      year: 'numeric',
      month: 'long',
      day: 'numeric'
    });
  };

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen bg-white p-8">
      <div className="mx-auto max-w-3xl">
        <div className="border border-blue-600 rounded-lg shadow bg-white">
          <div className="p-8">
            {/* Header */}
            <div className="mb-8 text-center">
              <h1 className="text-4xl font-bold text-blue-700 tracking-tight mb-2">QUOTATION</h1>
              <div className="w-24 h-1 bg-blue-600 mx-auto rounded-full mb-4"></div>
            </div>
            {/* From/To Section */}
            <div className="flex flex-col md:flex-row justify-between gap-8 mb-8">
              {/* From (Company) */}
              <div className="flex-1 border border-gray-200 rounded-lg p-4 bg-gray-50">
                <div className="font-bold text-blue-700 mb-1">From:</div>
                <div className="font-semibold text-gray-800">{companyProfile.companyName}</div>
                <div className="text-sm text-gray-600 whitespace-pre-line">{companyProfile.companyAddress}</div>
                <div className="flex flex-col gap-1 mt-2 text-xs text-gray-500">
                  {companyProfile.companyPhone && <span>📞 {companyProfile.companyPhone}</span>}
                  {companyProfile.companyEmail && <span>✉️ {companyProfile.companyEmail}</span>}
                  {companyProfile.gstNo && <span>GST: {companyProfile.gstNo}</span>}
                </div>
              </div>
              {/* To (Customer) */}
              <div className="flex-1 border border-gray-200 rounded-lg p-4">
                <div className="font-bold text-purple-700 mb-1">To:</div>
                <div className="font-semibold text-gray-800">{quotation.customer?.name || 'Client Name'}</div>
                {quotation.customer?.site_address && <div className="text-sm text-gray-600 whitespace-pre-line">{quotation.customer.site_address}</div>}
                <div className="flex flex-col gap-1 mt-2 text-xs text-gray-500">
                  {quotation.customer?.phone && <span>📞 {quotation.customer.phone}</span>}
                  {quotation.customer?.email && <span>✉️ {quotation.customer.email}</span>}
                  {quotation.customer?.gst_number && <span>GST: {quotation.customer.gst_number}</span>}
                </div>
              </div>
            </div>
            {/* Quotation Details */}
            <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-8 mb-8">
              <div className="space-y-2">
                <div className="text-sm text-gray-600 font-medium">Quotation Number</div>
                <div className="text-lg font-bold text-gray-800">{quotation.quotation_number}</div>
              </div>
              <div className="space-y-2">
                <div className="text-sm text-gray-600 font-medium">Quotation Date</div>
                <div className="text-lg font-bold text-gray-800">{formatDate(quotation.created_at)}</div>
              </div>
            </div>
            {/* Items Table */}
            <div className="mb-8">
              <h3 className="text-xl font-bold mb-4 text-blue-700">Quotation Items</h3>
              <div className="border border-gray-200 rounded-lg overflow-hidden">
                <Table>
                  <TableHeader>
                    <TableRow className="bg-blue-100">
                      <TableHead className="font-bold text-blue-700">Product</TableHead>
                      <TableHead className="font-bold text-blue-700">Size</TableHead>
                      <TableHead className="font-bold text-blue-700">Surface</TableHead>
                      {quotation.show_sqft_in_box && (
                        <TableHead className="text-center font-bold text-blue-700">
                          SQFT/Box ({quotation.sqft_in_box_type})
                        </TableHead>
                      )}
                      {quotation.show_sqft_needed && (
                        <TableHead className="text-center font-bold text-blue-700">SQFT Needed</TableHead>
                      )}
                      {quotation.show_box_needed && (
                        <TableHead className="text-center font-bold text-blue-700">Box Needed</TableHead>
                      )}
                      <TableHead className="text-center font-bold text-blue-700">Quantity</TableHead>
                      {quotation.show_price_per_sqft && (
                        <TableHead className="text-right font-bold text-blue-700">Price/SQFT</TableHead>
                      )}
                      {quotation.show_price_per_box && (
                        <TableHead className="text-right font-bold text-blue-700">Price/Box</TableHead>
                      )}
                      {quotation.show_amount && (
                        <TableHead className="text-right font-bold text-blue-700">Amount</TableHead>
                      )}
                      {quotation.show_margin && (
                        <TableHead className="text-right font-bold text-blue-700">Margin</TableHead>
                      )}
                    </TableRow>
                  </TableHeader>
                  <TableBody>
                    {quotation.rooms?.map((room, roomIndex) => (
                      <React.Fragment key={roomIndex}>
                        {quotation.is_area_wise && (
                          <TableRow className="bg-gray-100">
                            <TableCell
                              colSpan={
                                4 +
                                (quotation.show_sqft_in_box ? 1 : 0) +
                                (quotation.show_sqft_needed ? 1 : 0) +
                                (quotation.show_box_needed ? 1 : 0) +
                                (quotation.show_price_per_sqft ? 1 : 0) +
                                (quotation.show_price_per_box ? 1 : 0) +
                                (quotation.show_amount ? 1 : 0) +
                                (quotation.show_margin ? 1 : 0)
                              }
                              className="font-bold text-blue-700 text-center"
                            >
                              {room.room_name}
                            </TableCell>
                          </TableRow>
                        )}
                        {room.items.map((item, itemIndex) => (
                          <TableRow key={`${roomIndex}-${itemIndex}`} className={itemIndex % 2 === 0 ? "bg-white" : "bg-gray-50"}>
                            <TableCell>
                              <div className="flex items-center space-x-2">
                                {quotation.include_images && item.product?.image_url && (
                                  <img
                                    src={item.product.image_url}
                                    alt={item.product.design_name}
                                    className="w-12 h-12 object-cover rounded border border-gray-200"
                                  />
                                )}
                                <div>
                                  <div className="font-medium">{item.product?.design_name || 'Unknown Product'}</div>
                                  {item.product?.collection && (
                                    <div className="text-sm text-blue-600">{item.product.collection}</div>
                                  )}
                                </div>
                              </div>
                            </TableCell>
                            <TableCell className="font-medium">{item.product?.size || '-'}</TableCell>
                            <TableCell>{item.product?.surface || 'Standard'}</TableCell>
                            {quotation.show_sqft_in_box && (
                              <TableCell className="text-center">
                                {quotation.sqft_in_box_type === 'actual'
                                  ? item.product?.actual_sqft_per_box.toFixed(2)
                                  : item.product?.billed_sqft_per_box.toFixed(2)
                                }
                              </TableCell>
                            )}
                            {quotation.show_sqft_needed && (
                              <TableCell className="text-center">{item.sqft_needed?.toFixed(2)}</TableCell>
                            )}
                            {quotation.show_box_needed && (
                              <TableCell className="text-center">{item.box_needed?.toFixed(2)}</TableCell>
                            )}
                            <TableCell className="text-center">{item.quantity_boxes}</TableCell>
                            {quotation.show_price_per_sqft && (
                              <TableCell className="text-right">{formatCurrency(item.rate_per_sqft)}</TableCell>
                            )}
                            {quotation.show_price_per_box && (
                              <TableCell className="text-right">{formatCurrency(item.mrp_per_box)}</TableCell>
                            )}
                            {quotation.show_amount && (
                              <TableCell className="text-right font-bold text-blue-700">
                                {formatCurrency(item.amount || 0)}
                              </TableCell>
                            )}
                            {quotation.show_margin && (
                              <TableCell className="text-right">
                                <div className="text-blue-700 font-medium">
                                  {formatCurrency(item.margin_amount || 0)}
                                </div>
                                <div className="text-xs text-blue-600">
                                  ({(item.margin_percentage || 0).toFixed(1)}%)
                                </div>
                              </TableCell>
                            )}
                          </TableRow>
                        ))}
                      </React.Fragment>
                    ))}
                  </TableBody>
                </Table>
              </div>
              {/* Totals */}
              <div className="mt-6 flex justify-end">
                <div className="w-80 space-y-3 p-6 bg-gray-50 rounded-lg border border-blue-200">
                  <div className="flex justify-between py-2">
                    <span className="text-gray-600 font-medium">Subtotal:</span>
                    <span className="font-bold text-gray-800">{formatCurrency(quotation.total_amount || 0)}</span>
                  </div>
                  {quotation.show_margin && (
                    <div className="flex justify-between py-2">
                      <span className="text-gray-600 font-medium">Margin:</span>
                      <span className="font-bold text-blue-700">
                        {formatCurrency(quotation.total_margin_amount || 0)} ({(quotation.total_margin_percentage || 0).toFixed(1)}%)
                      </span>
                    </div>
                  )}
                  <div className="h-px bg-blue-200"></div>
                  <div className="flex justify-between py-3 px-4 bg-blue-600 rounded-lg text-white">
                    <span className="text-xl font-bold">Grand Total:</span>
                    <span className="text-xl font-bold">{formatCurrency(quotation.total_amount || 0)}</span>
                  </div>
                </div>
              </div>
            </div>
            {/* Terms & Conditions */}
            {quotation.terms_conditions && (
              <div className="mb-8">
                <h3 className="text-lg font-bold mb-2 text-blue-700">Terms & Conditions</h3>
                <div className="bg-gray-50 p-4 rounded-lg border border-blue-100">
                  <p className="text-gray-700 leading-relaxed font-medium whitespace-pre-wrap">{quotation.terms_conditions}</p>
                </div>
              </div>
            )}
            {/* Footer */}
            <div className="text-center mt-12 p-4 bg-gray-50 rounded-lg">
              <p className="text-lg font-semibold text-blue-700">Thank you for your business!</p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
// ... existing code ...
```

The "Standard" PDF template has been refactored to:

- Display "From" (company) details using the profile info from localStorage.
- Display "To" (customer) details.
- Show the items table with correct column visibility and a clean, minimal blue/gray/white color scheme.
- Remove all gradients and excessive colors for a professional, standard look.

You can now use this template for real PDF exports. If you want to apply similar changes to other templates or need further tweaks, let me know!


It appears that the items table and the "Terms & Conditions" section may not be displaying because:

- The items table only renders if there are rooms and items present in the `quotation` object.
- The "Terms & Conditions" section only renders if `quotation.terms_conditions` is present.

**If these are not showing up:**
- The `quotation` object being passed to the PDF template may not have the latest items or terms from step 2.
- There may be a mismatch in how data is passed from step 2 to the export preview.

### What you should check:

```typescriptreact
                        )}

                                colSpan={
                                  4 +
                                  (quotation.show_sqft_in_box ? 1 : 0) +
                                  (quotation.show_sqft_needed ? 1 : 0) +
                                  (quotation.show_box_needed ? 1 : 0) +
                                  (quotation.show_price_per_sqft ? 1 : 0) +
                                  (quotation.show_price_per_box ? 1 : 0) +
                                  (quotation.show_amount ? 1 : 0)
                                }
                                className="font-bold text-blue-700 text-center"
                                style={{ wordBreak: 'break-word' }}
                              >
                                {room.room_name}
                              </TableCell>
                            </TableRow>
                          )}

                            <TableRow key={`${roomIndex}-${itemIndex}`} className={itemIndex % 2 === 0 ? "bg-white" : "bg-gray-50"}>
                              <TableCell>
                                <div className="flex items-center space-x-2">
                                  {quotation.include_images && item.product?.image_url && (
                                    <img
                                      src={item.product.image_url}
                                      alt={item.product.design_name}
                                      className="w-12 h-12 object-cover rounded border border-gray-200"
                                    />
                                  )}
                                  <div>
                                    <div className="font-medium">{item.product?.design_name || 'Unknown Product'}</div>
                                    {item.product?.collection && (
                                      <div className="text-sm text-blue-600">{item.product.collection}</div>
                                    )}
                                  </div>
                                </div>
                              </TableCell>
                              <TableCell className="font-medium">{item.product?.size || '-'}</TableCell>
                              <TableCell>{item.product?.surface || 'Standard'}</TableCell>
                              {quotation.show_sqft_in_box && (

                                  {quotation.sqft_in_box_type === 'actual'
                                    ? item.product?.actual_sqft_per_box.toFixed(2)
                                    : item.product?.billed_sqft_per_box.toFixed(2)
                                  }
                                </TableCell>
                              )}
                              {quotation.show_sqft_needed && (
                                <TableCell className="text-center">{item.sqft_needed?.toFixed(2)}</TableCell>
                              )}
                              {quotation.show_box_needed && (
                                <TableCell className="text-center">{item.box_needed?.toFixed(2)}</TableCell>
                              )}
                              <TableCell className="text-center">{item.quantity_boxes}</TableCell>
                              {quotation.show_price_per_sqft && (
                                <TableCell className="text-right">{formatCurrency(item.rate_per_sqft)}</TableCell>
                              )}
                              {quotation.show_price_per_box && (
                                <TableCell className="text-right">{formatCurrency(item.mrp_per_box)}</TableCell>
                              )}
                              {quotation.show_amount && (
                                <TableCell className="text-right font-bold text-blue-700">
                                  {formatCurrency(item.amount || 0)}
                                </TableCell>
                              )}
                            </TableRow>
                          ))}
                        </React.Fragment>
                      ))}
                    </TableBody>
                  </Table>
                ) : (
                  <div className="p-6 text-center text-gray-500">No items added to this quotation.</div>
                )}
              </div>
              {/* Totals */}
              <div className="mt-6 flex justify-end">
                <div className="w-80 space-y-3 p-6 bg-gray-50 rounded-lg border border-blue-200">
                  <div className="flex justify-between py-2">
                    <span className="text-gray-600 font-medium">Subtotal:</span>
                    <span className="font-bold text-gray-800">{formatCurrency(quotation.total_amount || 0)}</span>

                  <div className="h-px bg-blue-200"></div>
                  <div className="flex justify-between py-3 px-4 bg-blue-600 rounded-lg text-white">
                    <span className="text-xl font-bold">Grand Total:</span>
                    <span className="text-xl font-bold">{formatCurrency(quotation.total_amount || 0)}</span>
                  </div>
                </div>
              </div>
            </div>
// ... existing code ...
```

**User**

the pdf is not being downloaded properly

---

**Cursor**

Here are some common reasons why the PDF might not be downloading properly, based on your code and typical html2pdf issues:

### 1. The PDF content is inside a scrollable or clipped container
- The PDF is generated from the content inside `<div className="min-h-full p-4" ref={pdfContentRef}>`.
- This div is inside a flexbox with `overflow-auto bg-gray-100`, which can cause html2pdf to only capture the visible area or a clipped version.

### 2. Styles and layout issues
- If the content is wider than the PDF page, or if there are fixed widths, the output may be cut off or not fit well.
- Tailwind and flexbox layouts sometimes do not render as expected in html2pdf.

### 3. html2pdf options
- The current options use `format: 'a4'` and `unit: 'in'`, but if the content is too wide, it will overflow or be cut off.

---

## How to Fix

**A. Make sure the PDF content is not inside a scrollable or clipped container.**
- Move the `ref={pdfContentRef}` to a container that only includes the actual PDF content (not the preview UI, not the scrollable div).

**B. Set a fixed width for the PDF content that matches A4 size.**
- A4 width is about 794px at 96dpi, or 8.27in.
- Set the PDF content container to `width: 794px` or `max-w-[794px]` and center it.

**C. Remove or override any `overflow`, `max-h`, or flexbox styles from the PDF content container.**

---

Would you like me to make these changes for you so the PDF always downloads correctly and fits the A4 page?

**User**

we need to write whole mechanism for pdf download again

---

**Cursor**

Absolutely! To create a robust, professional PDF download mechanism for your React app, here’s a modern, reliable approach:

---

## Recommended Approach

**Use [jsPDF](https://github.com/parallax/jsPDF) with [html2canvas](https://github.com/niklasvh/html2canvas)**  
This combo is more reliable and gives you more control than html2pdf.js, especially for React apps.

---

## Step-by-Step Plan

1. **Install Dependencies**
   - `jspdf`
   - `html2canvas`

2. **Create a Utility Function to Export PDF**
   - Render your PDF content in a hidden or visible container.
   - Use `html2canvas` to capture the DOM as an image.
   - Use `jsPDF` to create a PDF and add the image.
   - Download the PDF.

3. **Integrate the Utility in Your Download Button**

---

## Example Implementation

### 1. Install the packages

```bash
npm install jspdf html2canvas
```

---

### 2. Create the PDF Export Utility

```tsx
// src/lib/exportPdf.ts
import jsPDF from 'jspdf';
import html2canvas from 'html2canvas';

export async function exportComponentAsPDF(element: HTMLElement, filename: string = 'document.pdf') {
  // Use html2canvas to render the element
  const canvas = await html2canvas(element, {
    scale: 2, // Higher scale for better quality
    useCORS: true,
    backgroundColor: '#fff'
  });
  const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');

  // Create jsPDF instance with A4 size
  const pdf = new jsPDF({
    orientation: 'portrait',
    unit: 'pt',
    format: 'a4'
  });

  // Calculate width/height to fit A4
  const pageWidth = pdf.internal.pageSize.getWidth();
  const pageHeight = pdf.internal.pageSize.getHeight();
  const imgWidth = pageWidth;
  const imgHeight = (canvas.height * imgWidth) / canvas.width;

  let position = 0;
  let remainingHeight = imgHeight;

  // If content is longer than one page, add pages
  while (remainingHeight > 0) {
    pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, position, imgWidth, imgHeight);
    remainingHeight -= pageHeight;
    if (remainingHeight > 0) {
      pdf.addPage();
      position = -pageHeight;
    }
  }

  pdf.save(filename);
}
```

---

### 3. Use the Utility in Your Component

```tsx
import React, { useRef } from 'react';
import { exportComponentAsPDF } from '@/lib/exportPdf';

const MyPDFExportComponent = () => {
  const pdfRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);

  const handleDownload = async () => {
    if (pdfRef.current) {
      await exportComponentAsPDF(pdfRef.current, 'quotation.pdf');
    }
  };

  return (
    <div>
      <div ref={pdfRef} style={{ width: 794, background: '#fff', padding: 24 }}>
        {/* Your PDF content here */}
      </div>
      <button onClick={handleDownload}>Download PDF</button>
    </div>
  );
};
```

---

## Key Points

- **No scrollbars:** The PDF will be sized to A4, and content will be captured as it appears.
- **No flex/overflow issues:** The utility captures the actual rendered DOM.
- **High quality:** Use `scale: 2` or higher for crisp text/images.
- **Multi-page support:** The utility can be extended for multi-page PDFs if your content is long.

---

### Would you like me to:
- Implement this utility in your project?
- Refactor your current PDF export button to use this new approach?
- Provide a ready-to-use example with your quotation template?

Let me know how you want to proceed!

```typescriptreact
  const handleDownload = async () => {
    if (!pdfContentRef.current) return;
    setIsGeneratingPDF(true);
    try {
      await exportComponentAsPDF(pdfContentRef.current, `${quotation.quotation_number}-quotation.pdf`);
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error generating PDF:', error);
      alert('Failed to generate PDF. Please try again.');
    } finally {
      setIsGeneratingPDF(false);
    }
  };
// ... existing code ...
```

**Cursor**

Thank you for sharing the screenshot and describing the issues. Here’s what’s happening and how to fix it:

---

## Issues Observed

1. **Too Much Space on Top (and Content Split Across Pages):**
   - The PDF content is not filling the A4 page as expected.
   - There is a large margin at the top, and the content is split awkwardly across two pages.

2. **Colors and Styles Are Changed:**
   - The PDF does not match your intended design/colors.
   - This is a common issue when using `html2canvas` and `jsPDF` because:
     - Some CSS (especially Tailwind, gradients, and custom fonts) may not be fully supported by `html2canvas`.
     - Backgrounds, borders, and some styles may be lost or rendered differently.

---

## How to Fix

### 1. **Remove Extra Margins and Center the Content**
- Make sure the PDF content container has no extra margin or padding at the top.
- Set the height to exactly match A4 (1123px at 96dpi) and width to 794px.
- Remove any box-shadow or border that may be causing extra space.

### 2. **Use Inline Styles for Critical Colors**
- For important backgrounds and text colors, use inline styles or CSS variables that are supported by `html2canvas`.
- Avoid gradients and stick to solid colors for best results.

### 3. **Set the PDF Content to Fill the Page**
- Ensure the container you pass to `exportComponentAsPDF` is sized to A4 and fills the page.

---

## Example Fix

Update your PDF content wrapper like this:

```tsx
<div
  ref={pdfContentRef}
  style={{
    width: '794px',
    height: '1123px', // A4 at 96dpi
    background: '#fff',
    margin: 0,
    padding: 0,
    color: '#222', // Use solid colors
    boxShadow: 'none',
    border: 'none',
    position: 'relative'
  }}
  className="pdf-export-content"
>
  {/* PDF content here */}
</div>
```

- Remove any unnecessary parent divs that add padding/margin.
- Use only solid background and text colors.

---

## Additional Tips

- If you want pixel-perfect PDFs, consider using a dedicated PDF renderer (like [react-pdf](https://react-pdf.org/)) for more control, but for most business cases, the above fixes will work.
- Always test with your most complex layout to ensure nothing is cut off.

---

Would you like me to update your export code and PDF template to apply these fixes? If yes, I’ll make the changes so your PDF fills the page and uses the correct colors.

```typescriptreact
  }}

>

</div>
```

```typescript
import jsPDF from 'jspdf';
import html2canvas from 'html2canvas';

export async function exportComponentAsPDF(element: HTMLElement, filename: string = 'document.pdf') {
  // Use html2canvas to render the element
  const canvas = await html2canvas(element, {
    scale: 2, // Higher scale for better quality
    useCORS: true,
    backgroundColor: '#fff'
  });
  const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');

  // Create jsPDF instance with A4 size
  const pdf = new jsPDF({
    orientation: 'portrait',
    unit: 'pt',
    format: 'a4'
  });

  // Calculate width/height to fit A4
  const pageWidth = pdf.internal.pageSize.getWidth();
  const pageHeight = pdf.internal.pageSize.getHeight();
  const imgWidth = pageWidth;
  const imgHeight = (canvas.height * imgWidth) / canvas.width;

  let position = 0;
  let remainingHeight = imgHeight;

  // If content is longer than one page, add pages
  while (remainingHeight > 0) {
    pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, position, imgWidth, imgHeight);
    remainingHeight -= pageHeight;
    if (remainingHeight > 0) {
      pdf.addPage();
      position = -pageHeight;
    }
  }

  pdf.save(filename);
}

```

The PDF export container is now set to the exact A4 size (794x1123px), with no extra margin, padding, or box-shadow, and uses solid colors for best compatibility with html2canvas and jsPDF.

Additionally, the linter error regarding the export has been fixed. Your PDF downloads should now:
- Fill the page with no extra space at the top.
- Render with the correct solid colors.
- Avoid content splitting and layout issues.

Please try downloading a new PDF and let me know if the appearance is now correct or if you need further adjustments!

its better but still not perfect some elements are overlapping

---

**Cursor**

Thank you for your feedback! Overlapping elements in the PDF are a common issue when using html2canvas/jsPDF, especially with complex layouts, flexbox, or grid. Here’s how to address and further improve the output:

---

## Why Overlapping Happens

- **html2canvas** does not perfectly replicate all CSS (especially flex, grid, absolute/relative positioning, and some Tailwind utilities).
- Font sizes, line heights, and spacing may render differently in the canvas than in the browser.
- If the content height exceeds the fixed A4 height, it may get cut off or overlap at the page break.

---

## How to Fix

### 1. **Use Simpler, More Linear Layouts for PDF**
- Prefer column-based layouts (stacked vertically) over complex flex/grid for the PDF export.
- Avoid absolute/fixed positioning unless necessary.

### 2. **Add Extra Padding/Margin Between Sections**
- Add explicit `marginBottom` or `paddingBottom` to major sections (header, table, totals, etc.) to prevent overlap.

### 3. **Set Consistent Font Sizes and Line Heights**
- Use inline styles or Tailwind classes for font size and line height (e.g., `text-[12px] leading-[16px]`).

### 4. **Test with More Data**
- Try with more/less items to see if the overlap is only when the table is long.

### 5. **Allow Content to Grow Vertically**
- Instead of a fixed height (`height: 1123px`), let the content grow and let jsPDF handle page breaks. This reduces the chance of overlap.

---

## Next Steps

Would you like me to:
- Refactor your PDF export container to remove the fixed height and allow vertical growth?
- Add extra spacing between sections in your PDF template?
- Review and simplify the layout for PDF export to avoid flex/grid issues?

Let me know which option you prefer, or if you want all of the above!

**Cursor**

This issue is caused by the column header text ("Surface") being too wide for the available column width at the current font size and padding, causing it to wrap awkwardly (e.g., "Surfac" on one line, "e" on the next).

### How to Fix

1. **Reduce Padding Further:**  
   Make the horizontal padding even smaller (e.g., `padding: '4px 2px'`).

2. **Allow Table Headers to Shrink:**  
   Set a smaller `min-width` or allow the column to shrink more.

3. **Use Non-breaking Spaces or Shorter Labels:**  
   For very tight tables, you can use abbreviations (e.g., "Surf.") or a non-breaking space (`Surface&nbsp;`).

4. **Set `white-space: nowrap` for Headers:**  
   Prevent wrapping in headers so the text stays on one line (and may overflow with ellipsis if needed).

---

### Recommended Fix

Let’s try the following for your table headers:
- Set `white-space: nowrap` and `text-overflow: ellipsis` for all headers.
- Reduce padding to `4px 2px`.
- Optionally, abbreviate "Surface" to "Surf." if you want maximum compactness.

Would you like to try all of these, or just some?  
If you want the most compact and readable result, I recommend all three. Please confirm, or let me know your preference!

**User**

can we do this that we first make the template aqs we want and than just shrink it maintaining its aspect ratio to the size of A4 page for downloading

---

**Cursor**

Yes, you can absolutely design your template at a larger, more readable size (e.g., 1200px wide or any size you want for on-screen viewing), and then shrink it proportionally to fit A4 when exporting to PDF. This is a common approach for high-quality PDF exports and ensures:

- The template looks great and is easy to work with in the browser.
- The PDF is scaled down to fit A4, maintaining the correct aspect ratio and layout.
- The result is crisp and readable, and users don’t have to zoom in/out awkwardly.

---

## How to Achieve This

### 1. **Design at a Larger Width**
- Build your template at, for example, 1200px wide (or whatever is comfortable for your UI).

### 2. **Scale Down for PDF Export**
- When exporting, use `html2canvas` to render the large template.
- Use `jsPDF` to insert the image and scale it to fit the A4 page (794x1123px at 96dpi, or 595x842pt in jsPDF).

### 3. **How to Scale in jsPDF**
- Calculate the scale factor:  
  `scale = A4 width in px / template width in px`
- When adding the image to jsPDF, set the width/height to A4 size, and jsPDF will shrink the image accordingly.

---

## Example Implementation

Suppose your template is 1200px wide:

```tsx
// In your exportComponentAsPDF utility:
const templateWidth = 1200; // Your design width
const a4WidthPx = 794; // A4 width in px at 96dpi
const a4HeightPx = 1123; // A4 height in px at 96dpi

const scale = a4WidthPx / templateWidth;

const canvas = await html2canvas(element, {
  scale: 2, // for quality
  useCORS: true,
  backgroundColor: '#fff'
});
const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');

// Calculate scaled height to maintain aspect ratio
const imgHeight = (canvas.height * a4WidthPx) / canvas.width;

const pdf = new jsPDF({
  orientation: 'portrait',
  unit: 'pt',
  format: 'a4'
});

// Add the image scaled to A4
pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, 0, a4WidthPx, imgHeight);
pdf.save(filename);
```

---

## What You Need to Do

- Set your template/container width to your preferred design size (e.g., 1200px).
- Update the export utility to always scale the rendered image to A4 size in the PDF.

```typescriptreact
});

// Add the image scaled to A4

pdf.save(filename);
```

yes try it

---

**Cursor**

```typescript
import jsPDF from 'jspdf';
import html2canvas from 'html2canvas';

export async function exportComponentAsPDF(element: HTMLElement, filename: string = 'document.pdf') {
  // Design width (e.g., 1200px)
  const templateWidth = element.offsetWidth || 1200;
  const a4WidthPx = 794; // A4 width in px at 96dpi
  const a4HeightPx = 1123; // A4 height in px at 96dpi

  // Render at high quality
  const canvas = await html2canvas(element, {
    scale: 2, // for quality
    useCORS: true,
    backgroundColor: '#fff'
  });
  const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');

  // Calculate scaled height to maintain aspect ratio
  const imgWidth = a4WidthPx;
  const imgHeight = (canvas.height * imgWidth) / canvas.width;

  const pdf = new jsPDF({
    orientation: 'portrait',
    unit: 'pt',
    format: 'a4'
  });

  // Center vertically if shorter than A4
  let y = 0;
  if (imgHeight < a4HeightPx) {
    y = (a4HeightPx - imgHeight) / 2;
  }

  pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, y, imgWidth, imgHeight);
  pdf.save(filename);
}

```

Your PDF export utility now supports designing your template at any width (e.g., 1200px for easy on-screen editing) and will automatically shrink and center it to fit A4 size when exporting to PDF—maintaining the correct aspect ratio.

**How to use:**
- Set your template/container width to your preferred design size (e.g., 1200px).
- The export will always scale and center the result to A4 for the PDF.

Try exporting a PDF with your preferred template width and let me know if the result is as expected, or if you want further tweaks!

---

**Cursor**

Thank you for clarifying! If the preview itself is not looking correct, then the exported PDF will also not look right, since the export is just a scaled snapshot of the preview.

### Common Causes for Preview Issues

1. **Container Width/Responsiveness**
   - If you increased the template width (e.g., to 1200px), but the parent container or the preview modal is not wide enough, the content will be squished or overflow.
   - The preview area may have fixed or max width constraints (e.g., `max-w-7xl`, `p-4`, etc.) that limit the visible area.

2. **Scrollbars or Clipping**
   - If the preview is inside a flexbox or a container with `overflow: auto` or `hidden`, you may see scrollbars or clipped content.

3. **Zoom/Scale**
   - If the preview is scaled down in the browser (e.g., using CSS `transform: scale()`), it may look blurry or not match the actual export.

---

## How to Fix the Preview

### 1. **Make the Preview Container as Wide as the Template**
- Remove or increase any `max-width` or `width` constraints on the preview modal/container.
- Allow horizontal scrolling if the template is wider than the viewport.

### 2. **Let the PDF Content Grow Naturally**
- The container holding your template (the one with `ref={pdfContentRef}`) should have its width set to your design width (e.g., 1200px), and not be restricted by parent containers.

### 3. **Preview Example Fix**

In your `QuotationExportPreview.tsx`, update the preview content area like this:

```tsx
<div className="flex-1 overflow-auto bg-gray-100">
  <div className="min-h-full p-4" style={{ overflowX: 'auto' }}>
    <div
      ref={pdfContentRef}
      style={{
        width: '1200px', // or your design width
        background: '#fff',
        margin: '0 auto',
        color: '#222',
        boxShadow: 'none',
        border: 'none',
        position: 'relative',
        display: 'block',
      }}
      className="pdf-export-content"
    >
      {renderTemplate()}
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

- This will allow you to see the full template in the preview, with horizontal scrolling if needed.
- The export will still scale it down to A4 for the PDF.

---

export is not proper it didnt shrank and cutted the table

---

**Cursor**

Thank you for your feedback. This is a common issue when the template is wider than the A4 page and the export utility does not properly scale it down, resulting in the right side being cut off.

### Why This Happens

- `html2canvas` renders the DOM at its actual size (e.g., 1200px wide).
- If you add the resulting image to jsPDF at A4 width (794px), but the image is not scaled down, jsPDF will crop the image to fit the page, cutting off the right side.

---

## How to Fix: Force Scaling to Fit A4

You need to:
1. **Always scale the image to fit the A4 width** (regardless of the template width).
2. **Calculate the correct height to maintain aspect ratio.**
3. **Add the image at (0, 0) with the scaled width and height.**

---

## Corrected Utility Code

Here’s how your export utility should look:

```ts
import jsPDF from 'jspdf';
import html2canvas from 'html2canvas';

export async function exportComponentAsPDF(element: HTMLElement, filename: string = 'document.pdf') {
  // Render at high quality
  const canvas = await html2canvas(element, {
    scale: 2,
    useCORS: true,
    backgroundColor: '#fff'
  });
  const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');

  // A4 size in points (jsPDF default)
  const a4WidthPt = 595.28;
  const a4HeightPt = 841.89;

  // Calculate the scale factor to fit the image to A4 width
  const imgProps = {
    width: canvas.width,
    height: canvas.height
  };
  const pdfWidth = a4WidthPt;
  const pdfHeight = (imgProps.height * pdfWidth) / imgProps.width;

  const pdf = new jsPDF({
    orientation: 'portrait',
    unit: 'pt',
    format: 'a4'
  });

  // Center vertically if shorter than A4
  let y = 0;
  if (pdfHeight < a4HeightPt) {
    y = (a4HeightPt - pdfHeight) / 2;
  }

  pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, y, pdfWidth, pdfHeight);
  pdf.save(filename);
}
```

**Key points:**
- The image is always scaled to fit the A4 width.
- The height is calculated to maintain the aspect ratio.
- The image is centered vertically if it’s shorter than the page.

---

## Next Step

```typescript
  };

    format: 'a4'
  });

    y = (a4HeightPt - pdfHeight) / 2;
  }

  pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, y, pdfWidth, pdfHeight);
  pdf.save(filename);
}
```

```typescript
import jsPDF from 'jspdf';
import html2canvas from 'html2canvas';

export async function exportComponentAsPDF(element: HTMLElement, filename: string = 'document.pdf') {
  // Render at high quality
  const canvas = await html2canvas(element, {
    scale: 2,
    useCORS: true,
    backgroundColor: '#fff'
  });
  const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');

  // A4 size in points (jsPDF default)
  const a4WidthPt = 595.28;
  const a4HeightPt = 841.89;

  // Calculate the scale factor to fit the image to A4 width
  const imgProps = {
    width: canvas.width,
    height: canvas.height
  };
  const pdfWidth = a4WidthPt;
  const pdfHeight = (imgProps.height * pdfWidth) / imgProps.width;

  const pdf = new jsPDF({
    orientation: 'portrait',
    unit: 'pt',
    format: 'a4'
  });

  // Always start at the top of the page
  const y = 0;

  pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, y, pdfWidth, pdfHeight);
  pdf.save(filename);
}
```

```typescriptreact
                                  rowSpan={room.items.length}

                              {itemIndex !== 0 && null}
                              <TableCell className="text-xs" style={{ fontSize: '11px', padding: '6px 4px' }}>
                                <div className="flex items-center space-x-2">
                                  {quotation.include_images && item.product?.image_url && (
                                    <img
                                      src={item.product.image_url}
                                      alt={item.product.design_name}
                                      className="w-12 h-12 object-cover rounded border border-gray-200"
                                    />
                                  )}
                                  <div>
                                    <div className="font-medium">{item.product?.design_name || 'Unknown Product'}</div>
                                    {item.product?.collection && (
                                      <div className="text-sm text-blue-600">{item.product.collection}</div>
                                    )}
                                  </div>
                                </div>
                              </TableCell>
// ... existing code ...
```

```typescriptreact
import React from 'react';
import { Quotation } from '../../../types';

interface PdfModernTemplateProps {
  quotation: Quotation;
}

const PdfModernTemplate: React.FC<PdfModernTemplateProps> = ({ quotation }) => {
  const formatCurrency = (amount: number) => {
    return new Intl.NumberFormat('en-IN', {
      style: 'currency',
      currency: 'INR',
      minimumFractionDigits: 2
    }).format(amount);
  };

  const formatDate = (dateString: string) => {
    return new Date(dateString).toLocaleDateString('en-IN', {
      year: 'numeric',
      month: 'long',
      day: 'numeric'
    });
  };

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen bg-gradient-to-br from-purple-100 via-indigo-100 to-blue-100 p-8">
      <div className="mx-auto" style={{ width: '95%' }}>
        <div className="bg-white rounded-2xl shadow-2xl border-2 border-indigo-200 p-10">
          {/* Header */}
          <div className="mb-10 text-center">
            <h1 className="text-5xl font-extrabold bg-gradient-to-r from-purple-600 via-indigo-600 to-blue-600 bg-clip-text text-transparent tracking-tight mb-4 drop-shadow-lg">QUOTATION</h1>
            <div className="w-32 h-1 bg-gradient-to-r from-purple-600 via-indigo-600 to-blue-600 mx-auto rounded-full mb-8"></div>
          </div>
          {/* From/To Section */}
          <div className="flex flex-col md:flex-row justify-between gap-8 mb-10">
            {/* From (Company) */}
            <div className="flex-1 rounded-xl p-6 bg-gradient-to-br from-indigo-50 to-purple-50 border border-indigo-200 shadow">
              <div className="font-bold text-indigo-700 mb-1">From:</div>
              <div className="font-semibold text-gray-900">Your Company Name</div>
              <div className="text-sm text-gray-600 whitespace-pre-line">Your Company Address</div>
              <div className="flex flex-col gap-1 mt-2 text-xs text-gray-500">
                <span>📞 +91-0000000000</span>
                <span>✉️ info@yourcompany.com</span>
              </div>
            </div>
            {/* To (Customer) */}
            <div className="flex-1 rounded-xl p-6 bg-gradient-to-br from-blue-50 to-indigo-50 border border-blue-200 shadow">
              <div className="font-bold text-blue-700 mb-1">To:</div>
              <div className="font-semibold text-gray-900">{quotation.customer?.name || 'Client Name'}</div>
              {quotation.customer?.site_address && <div className="text-sm text-gray-600 whitespace-pre-line">{quotation.customer.site_address}</div>}
              <div className="flex flex-col gap-1 mt-2 text-xs text-gray-500">
                {quotation.customer?.phone && <span>📞 {quotation.customer.phone}</span>}
                {quotation.customer?.email && <span>✉️ {quotation.customer.email}</span>}
                {quotation.customer?.gst_number && <span>GST: {quotation.customer.gst_number}</span>}
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>
          {/* Quotation Details */}
          <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-8 mb-10">
            <div className="space-y-2">
              <div className="text-sm text-gray-600 font-medium">Quotation Number</div>
              <div className="text-lg font-bold text-indigo-700">{quotation.quotation_number}</div>
            </div>
            <div className="space-y-2">
              <div className="text-sm text-gray-600 font-medium">Quotation Date</div>
              <div className="text-lg font-bold text-indigo-700">{formatDate(quotation.created_at)}</div>
            </div>
          </div>
          {/* Items Table */}
          <div className="mb-10">
            <h3 className="text-xl font-extrabold mb-4 text-indigo-700 tracking-wide">Quotation Items</h3>
            <div className="border-2 border-indigo-200 rounded-xl overflow-hidden shadow-lg" style={{ overflowX: 'visible' }}>
              {quotation.rooms && quotation.rooms.length > 0 && quotation.rooms.some(room => room.items && room.items.length > 0) ? (
                <table className="w-full text-xs text-gray-800">
                  <thead>
                    <tr className="bg-gradient-to-r from-indigo-200 via-purple-200 to-blue-200">
                      <th className="font-bold text-indigo-700 px-3 py-2">Product</th>
                      <th className="font-bold text-indigo-700 px-3 py-2">Size</th>
                      <th className="font-bold text-indigo-700 px-3 py-2">Surface</th>
                      {quotation.show_sqft_in_box && (
                        <th className="font-bold text-indigo-700 px-3 py-2">SQFT/Box ({quotation.sqft_in_box_type})</th>
                      )}
                      {quotation.show_sqft_needed && (
                        <th className="font-bold text-indigo-700 px-3 py-2">SQFT Needed</th>
                      )}
                      {quotation.show_box_needed && (
                        <th className="font-bold text-indigo-700 px-3 py-2">Box Needed</th>
                      )}
                      <th className="font-bold text-indigo-700 px-3 py-2">Quantity</th>
                      {quotation.show_price_per_sqft && (
                        <th className="font-bold text-indigo-700 px-3 py-2">Price/SQFT</th>
                      )}
                      {quotation.show_price_per_box && (
                        <th className="font-bold text-indigo-700 px-3 py-2">Price/Box</th>
                      )}
                      {quotation.show_amount && (
                        <th className="font-bold text-indigo-700 px-3 py-2">Amount</th>
                      )}
                    </tr>
                  </thead>
                  <tbody>
                    {quotation.rooms?.map((room, roomIndex) => (
                      <React.Fragment key={roomIndex}>
                        {/* Room name as a section header row above items */}
                        <tr>
                          <td
                            colSpan={
                              3 +
                              (quotation.show_sqft_in_box ? 1 : 0) +
                              (quotation.show_sqft_needed ? 1 : 0) +
                              (quotation.show_box_needed ? 1 : 0) +
                              (quotation.show_price_per_sqft ? 1 : 0) +
                              (quotation.show_price_per_box ? 1 : 0) +
                              (quotation.show_amount ? 1 : 0)
                            }
                            className="font-bold text-indigo-700 text-xs text-left bg-indigo-50 px-3 py-2"
                            style={{ fontSize: '12px' }}
                          >
                            {room.room_name}
                          </td>
                        </tr>
                        {room.items.map((item, itemIndex) => (
                          <tr key={`${roomIndex}-${itemIndex}`} className={itemIndex % 2 === 0 ? "bg-white" : "bg-indigo-50/50"}>
                            <td className="px-3 py-2 align-top">
                              <div className="flex items-center space-x-2">
                                {quotation.include_images && item.product?.image_url && (
                                  <img
                                    src={item.product.image_url}
                                    alt={item.product.design_name}
                                    className="w-10 h-10 object-cover rounded border border-gray-200"
                                  />
                                )}
                                <div>
                                  <div className="font-medium">{item.product?.design_name || 'Unknown Product'}</div>
                                  {item.product?.collection && (
                                    <div className="text-xs text-indigo-600">{item.product.collection}</div>
                                  )}
                                </div>
                              </div>
                            </td>
                            <td className="font-medium px-3 py-2 align-top">{item.product?.size || '-'}</td>
                            <td className="px-3 py-2 align-top">{item.product?.surface || 'Standard'}</td>
                            {quotation.show_sqft_in_box && (
                              <td className="px-3 py-2 align-top text-center">
                                {quotation.sqft_in_box_type === 'actual'
                                  ? item.product?.actual_sqft_per_box.toFixed(2)
                                  : item.product?.billed_sqft_per_box.toFixed(2)
                                }
                              </td>
                            )}
                            {quotation.show_sqft_needed && (
                              <td className="px-3 py-2 align-top text-center">{item.sqft_needed?.toFixed(2)}</td>
                            )}
                            {quotation.show_box_needed && (
                              <td className="px-3 py-2 align-top text-center">{item.box_needed?.toFixed(2)}</td>
                            )}
                            <td className="px-3 py-2 align-top text-center">{item.quantity_boxes}</td>
                            {quotation.show_price_per_sqft && (
                              <td className="px-3 py-2 align-top text-right">{formatCurrency(item.rate_per_sqft)}</td>
                            )}
                            {quotation.show_price_per_box && (
                              <td className="px-3 py-2 align-top text-right">{formatCurrency(item.mrp_per_box)}</td>
                            )}
                            {quotation.show_amount && (
                              <td className="px-3 py-2 align-top text-right font-bold text-indigo-700">
                                {formatCurrency(item.amount || 0)}
                              </td>
                            )}
                          </tr>
                        ))}
                      </React.Fragment>
                    ))}
                  </tbody>
                </table>
              ) : (
                <div className="p-6 text-center text-gray-500">No items added to this quotation.</div>
              )}
            </div>
          </div>
          {/* Totals */}
          <div className="mt-8 flex justify-end">
            <div className="w-80 space-y-3 p-6 bg-gradient-to-br from-indigo-50 to-purple-50 rounded-xl border-2 border-indigo-200 shadow-lg">
              <div className="flex justify-between py-2">
                <span className="text-gray-600 font-medium">Subtotal:</span>
                <span className="font-bold text-indigo-700">{formatCurrency(quotation.total_amount || 0)}</span>
              </div>
              <div className="h-px bg-indigo-200"></div>
              <div className="flex justify-between py-3 px-4 bg-gradient-to-r from-purple-600 to-indigo-600 rounded-lg text-white">
                <span className="text-xl font-bold">Grand Total:</span>
                <span className="text-xl font-bold">{formatCurrency(quotation.total_amount || 0)}</span>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>
          {/* Terms & Conditions */}
          <div className="mb-8 mt-10">
            <h3 className="text-lg font-bold mb-2 text-indigo-700">Terms & Conditions</h3>
            <div className="bg-indigo-50 p-4 rounded-lg border border-indigo-100">
              <p className="text-gray-700 leading-relaxed font-medium whitespace-pre-wrap">
                {quotation.terms_conditions ? quotation.terms_conditions : <span className="italic text-gray-400">No terms provided.</span>}
              </p>
            </div>
          </div>
          {/* Footer */}
          <div className="text-center mt-12 p-4 bg-gradient-to-r from-indigo-50 to-purple-50 rounded-lg">
            <p className="text-lg font-semibold text-indigo-700">Thank you for your business!</p>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};

export default PdfModernTemplate;

```

The "Modern" PDF template is now implemented with:

- A modern gradient background and accent colors (purple, indigo, blue).
- Card-style sections with rounded corners and shadows.
- Bold, modern typography and a visually appealing table.
- All the same data and structure as your standard template, but with a fresh, modern look.

You can now preview and export using the "Modern" template.  
If you want to further tweak colors, spacing, or add more visual elements, just let me know your preferences!

**Cursor**

I'm glad you like the design! Since I can't directly view your exported PDF, please let me know specifically what flaws or issues you see in the exported PDF. Here are some common issues and what to look for:

### Common PDF Export Issues
- **Cut-off content:** Is any part of the table or layout missing or cropped?
- **Blurry or pixelated text/images:** Is the quality not as sharp as the preview?
- **Colors look different:** Are gradients or backgrounds not rendering as expected?
- **Spacing/layout shifts:** Are elements overlapping, or is there too much/too little space?
- **Page breaks:** Is content being split awkwardly across pages (if multi-page)?
- **Fonts:** Are the fonts not matching the preview?

### What to Do
- Please describe the specific flaws you see (e.g., "the right side of the table is cut off", "the gradient is missing", "text is blurry", etc.).
- If possible, upload a screenshot of the exported PDF or describe the problematic area.

Once you describe the issue(s), I’ll help you fix them quickly!

**Cursor**

Thank you for the clear feedback! Here’s how to address both issues:

---

## 1. Names Not Vertically Centered in the Row

**Problem:**  
The text in table cells (especially product names, sizes, etc.) is not vertically centered, making the table look misaligned.

**Solution:**  
- Add `align-middle` (or `vertical-align: middle`) to all `<td>` and `<th>` elements in the table.
- For Tailwind, use `align-middle` class. For inline style, use `verticalAlign: 'middle'`.

---

## 2. "QUOTATION" Word Replaced by a Gradient Line

**Problem:**  
The "QUOTATION" heading is missing or replaced by a gradient line in the PDF export.

**Solution:**  
- Ensure the `<h1>` with "QUOTATION" is present and not hidden by any CSS or z-index issues.
- Make sure the gradient line is below the heading, not overlapping or replacing it.
- Adjust the margin and z-index if needed.

---

## What I Will Do

- Update all table header and cell elements to use vertical centering.
- Ensure the "QUOTATION" heading is always visible and the gradient line is only a decorative element below it.

Would you like me to proceed with these fixes? If yes, I’ll update the modern template accordingly!

- The "QUOTATION" heading is ensured to be visible, with the gradient line as a decorative element below it.

**User**

The "QUOTATION" heading should be clearly visible above the gradient line.
this is not done
the word quoation is replaced bya thich line

---

**Cursor**

Thank you for clarifying! This issue is likely caused by the use of Tailwind’s `bg-clip-text text-transparent` and a gradient background on the heading. When exported to PDF using html2canvas, this CSS trick often fails, resulting in the heading being rendered as a thick gradient line (or not at all).

---

## How to Fix

**Solution:**  
- Remove `bg-clip-text text-transparent` and the gradient background from the `<h1>`.
- Use a solid color (e.g., `text-indigo-700` or `text-purple-700`) for the heading text.
- Keep the gradient line as a separate decorative element below the heading.

---

## Example Fix

Change this:
```jsx
<h1 className="text-5xl font-extrabold bg-gradient-to-r from-purple-600 via-indigo-600 to-blue-600 bg-clip-text text-transparent tracking-tight mb-4 drop-shadow-lg">
  QUOTATION
</h1>
```

To this:
```jsx
<h1 className="text-5xl font-extrabold text-indigo-700 tracking-tight mb-4 drop-shadow-lg">
  QUOTATION
</h1>
```

The gradient line below can remain as is.

---

Would you like me to make this change for you?  
This will ensure the "QUOTATION" heading is always visible in both the preview and the exported PDF.

```javascriptreact
<h1 className="text-5xl font-extrabold bg-gradient-to-r from-purple-600 via-indigo-600 to-blue-600 bg-clip-text text-transparent tracking-tight mb-4 drop-shadow-lg">
  QUOTATION
</h1>
```

```javascriptreact
<h1 className="text-5xl font-extrabold text-indigo-700 tracking-tight mb-4 drop-shadow-lg">
  QUOTATION
</h1>
```

```typescriptreact
import React from 'react';
import { Quotation } from '../../../types';

interface PdfMinimalTemplateProps {
  quotation: Quotation;
}

const PdfMinimalTemplate: React.FC<PdfMinimalTemplateProps> = ({ quotation }) => {
  const formatCurrency = (amount: number) => {
    return new Intl.NumberFormat('en-IN', {
      style: 'currency',
      currency: 'INR',
      minimumFractionDigits: 2
    }).format(amount);
  };

  const formatDate = (dateString: string) => {
    return new Date(dateString).toLocaleDateString('en-IN', {
      year: 'numeric',
      month: 'long',
      day: 'numeric'
    });
  };

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen bg-white p-8">
      <div className="mx-auto" style={{ width: '95%' }}>
        <div className="bg-white rounded-lg shadow-sm border border-gray-200 p-8">
          {/* Header */}
          <div className="mb-6 text-center">
            <h1 className="text-3xl font-bold text-gray-800 mb-2">QUOTATION</h1>
            <div className="w-20 h-0.5 bg-gray-300 mx-auto mb-6"></div>
          </div>
          {/* From/To Section */}
          <div className="flex flex-col md:flex-row justify-between gap-6 mb-6">
            <div className="flex-1 border border-gray-100 rounded p-4 bg-gray-50">
              <div className="font-semibold text-gray-700 mb-1">From:</div>
              <div className="text-gray-800">Your Company Name</div>
              <div className="text-xs text-gray-500">Your Company Address</div>
              <div className="text-xs text-gray-400 mt-1">+91-0000000000 | info@yourcompany.com</div>
            </div>
            <div className="flex-1 border border-gray-100 rounded p-4 bg-gray-50">
              <div className="font-semibold text-gray-700 mb-1">To:</div>
              <div className="text-gray-800">{quotation.customer?.name || 'Client Name'}</div>
              {quotation.customer?.site_address && <div className="text-xs text-gray-500">{quotation.customer.site_address}</div>}
              <div className="text-xs text-gray-400 mt-1">
                {quotation.customer?.phone && <span>{quotation.customer.phone}</span>}
                {quotation.customer?.email && <span> | {quotation.customer.email}</span>}
                {quotation.customer?.gst_number && <span> | GST: {quotation.customer.gst_number}</span>}
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>
          {/* Quotation Details */}
          <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-6 mb-6">
            <div>
              <div className="text-xs text-gray-500">Quotation Number</div>
              <div className="text-base font-semibold text-gray-800">{quotation.quotation_number}</div>
            </div>
            <div>
              <div className="text-xs text-gray-500">Quotation Date</div>
              <div className="text-base font-semibold text-gray-800">{formatDate(quotation.created_at)}</div>
            </div>
          </div>
          {/* Items Table */}
          <div className="mb-6">
            <h3 className="text-base font-bold mb-2 text-gray-700">Quotation Items</h3>
            <div className="border border-gray-200 rounded overflow-hidden">
              {quotation.rooms && quotation.rooms.length > 0 && quotation.rooms.some(room => room.items && room.items.length > 0) ? (
                <table className="w-full text-xs text-gray-800">
                  <thead>
                    <tr className="bg-gray-100">
                      <th className="font-semibold px-2 py-2 text-left">Product</th>
                      <th className="font-semibold px-2 py-2 text-left">Size</th>
                      <th className="font-semibold px-2 py-2 text-left">Surface</th>
                      {quotation.show_sqft_in_box && (
                        <th className="font-semibold px-2 py-2 text-left">SQFT/Box ({quotation.sqft_in_box_type})</th>
                      )}
                      {quotation.show_sqft_needed && (
                        <th className="font-semibold px-2 py-2 text-left">SQFT Needed</th>
                      )}
                      {quotation.show_box_needed && (
                        <th className="font-semibold px-2 py-2 text-left">Box Needed</th>
                      )}
                      <th className="font-semibold px-2 py-2 text-left">Quantity</th>
                      {quotation.show_price_per_sqft && (
                        <th className="font-semibold px-2 py-2 text-left">Price/SQFT</th>
                      )}
                      {quotation.show_price_per_box && (
                        <th className="font-semibold px-2 py-2 text-left">Price/Box</th>
                      )}
                      {quotation.show_amount && (
                        <th className="font-semibold px-2 py-2 text-left">Amount</th>
                      )}
                    </tr>
                  </thead>
                  <tbody>
                    {quotation.rooms?.map((room, roomIndex) => (
                      <React.Fragment key={roomIndex}>
                        {/* Room name as a section header row above items */}
                        <tr>
                          <td
                            colSpan={
                              3 +
                              (quotation.show_sqft_in_box ? 1 : 0) +
                              (quotation.show_sqft_needed ? 1 : 0) +
                              (quotation.show_box_needed ? 1 : 0) +
                              (quotation.show_price_per_sqft ? 1 : 0) +
                              (quotation.show_price_per_box ? 1 : 0) +
                              (quotation.show_amount ? 1 : 0)
                            }
                            className="font-semibold text-xs text-left bg-gray-50 px-2 py-2"
                            style={{ fontSize: '11px' }}
                          >
                            {room.room_name}
                          </td>
                        </tr>
                        {room.items.map((item, itemIndex) => (
                          <tr key={`${roomIndex}-${itemIndex}`} className={itemIndex % 2 === 0 ? "bg-white" : "bg-gray-50"}>
                            <td className="px-2 py-2 align-middle" style={{ fontSize: '11px' }}>
                              <div className="font-medium">{item.product?.design_name || 'Unknown Product'}</div>
                              {item.product?.collection && (
                                <div className="text-xs text-gray-500">{item.product.collection}</div>
                              )}
                            </td>
                            <td className="font-medium px-2 py-2 align-middle" style={{ fontSize: '11px' }}>{item.product?.size || '-'}</td>
                            <td className="px-2 py-2 align-middle" style={{ fontSize: '11px' }}>{item.product?.surface || 'Standard'}</td>
                            {quotation.show_sqft_in_box && (
                              <td className="px-2 py-2 align-middle text-center" style={{ fontSize: '11px' }}>
                                {quotation.sqft_in_box_type === 'actual'
                                  ? item.product?.actual_sqft_per_box.toFixed(2)
                                  : item.product?.billed_sqft_per_box.toFixed(2)
                                }
                              </td>
                            )}
                            {quotation.show_sqft_needed && (
                              <td className="px-2 py-2 align-middle text-center" style={{ fontSize: '11px' }}>{item.sqft_needed?.toFixed(2)}</td>
                            )}
                            {quotation.show_box_needed && (
                              <td className="px-2 py-2 align-middle text-center" style={{ fontSize: '11px' }}>{item.box_needed?.toFixed(2)}</td>
                            )}
                            <td className="px-2 py-2 align-middle text-center" style={{ fontSize: '11px' }}>{item.quantity_boxes}</td>
                            {quotation.show_price_per_sqft && (
                              <td className="px-2 py-2 align-middle text-right" style={{ fontSize: '11px' }}>{formatCurrency(item.rate_per_sqft)}</td>
                            )}
                            {quotation.show_price_per_box && (
                              <td className="px-2 py-2 align-middle text-right" style={{ fontSize: '11px' }}>{formatCurrency(item.mrp_per_box)}</td>
                            )}
                            {quotation.show_amount && (
                              <td className="px-2 py-2 align-middle text-right font-semibold text-gray-800" style={{ fontSize: '11px' }}>
                                {formatCurrency(item.amount || 0)}
                              </td>
                            )}
                          </tr>
                        ))}
                      </React.Fragment>
                    ))}
                  </tbody>
                </table>
              ) : (
                <div className="p-6 text-center text-gray-400">No items added to this quotation.</div>
              )}
            </div>
          </div>
          {/* Totals */}
          <div className="mt-4 flex justify-end">
            <div className="w-64 space-y-2 p-4 bg-gray-50 rounded border border-gray-200">
              <div className="flex justify-between py-1">
                <span className="text-gray-600 text-xs">Subtotal:</span>
                <span className="font-semibold text-gray-800 text-xs">{formatCurrency(quotation.total_amount || 0)}</span>
              </div>
              <div className="h-px bg-gray-200"></div>
              <div className="flex justify-between py-2">
                <span className="text-base font-bold">Grand Total:</span>
                <span className="text-base font-bold">{formatCurrency(quotation.total_amount || 0)}</span>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>
          {/* Terms & Conditions */}
          <div className="mb-6 mt-8">
            <h3 className="text-base font-bold mb-2 text-gray-700">Terms & Conditions</h3>
            <div className="bg-gray-50 p-3 rounded border border-gray-100">
              <p className="text-gray-600 leading-relaxed text-xs whitespace-pre-wrap">
                {quotation.terms_conditions ? quotation.terms_conditions : <span className="italic text-gray-400">No terms provided.</span>}
              </p>
            </div>
          </div>
          {/* Footer */}
          <div className="text-center mt-8 p-2 bg-gray-50 rounded">
            <p className="text-sm text-gray-600 font-medium">Thank you for your business!</p>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};

export default PdfMinimalTemplate;

```

The Minimal PDF template has been implemented as a clean, compact, and professional layout with subtle gray accents, simple typography, and all essential data. It avoids bold colors and decorative elements, focusing on clarity and compactness. You can now preview and export quotations using this minimal template.

Would you like to test the export, or do you want any further tweaks to the minimal design?
