```typescript
// Ensure this file is recognized as a module
export {}; 

import jsPDF from 'jspdf';
import html2canvas from 'html2canvas';

export async function exportComponentAsPDF(element: HTMLElement, filename: string = 'document.pdf') {
  try {
    console.log('=== PDF Export Started ===');
    console.log('Original element:', element);
    console.log('Element dimensions:', element.offsetWidth, 'x', element.offsetHeight);
    console.log('Element scroll dimensions:', element.scrollWidth, 'x', element.scrollHeight);
    
    // A4 size in points (jsPDF default)
    const a4WidthPt = 595.28;
    const a4HeightPt = 841.89;

    const pdf = new jsPDF({
      orientation: 'portrait',
      unit: 'pt',
      format: 'a4'
    });

    // Test with a simple approach first
    await testSimpleExport(element, pdf, a4WidthPt, a4HeightPt);
    pdf.save(filename);
    
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('PDF generation failed:', error);
    throw new Error('Failed to generate PDF. Please try again.');
  }
}

async function testSimpleExport(element: HTMLElement, pdf: jsPDF, a4WidthPt: number, a4HeightPt: number) {
  console.log('=== Testing Simple Export ===');
  
  // First, let's try with the original element without cloning
  console.log('Testing with original element...');
  
  // Make sure element is visible and has content
  const originalDisplay = element.style.display;
  const originalVisibility = element.style.visibility;
  const originalPosition = element.style.position;
  
  element.style.display = 'block';
  element.style.visibility = 'visible';
  element.style.position = 'relative';
  element.style.backgroundColor = '#ffffff';
  
  try {
    // Wait for any layout changes
    await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 500));
    
    console.log('Element styles applied');
    console.log('Element after styling:', element);
    console.log('Element dimensions after styling:', element.offsetWidth, 'x', element.offsetHeight);
    
    // Check if element has content
    const hasContent = element.innerHTML.length > 0;
    console.log('Element has content:', hasContent, 'Length:', element.innerHTML.length);
    
    if (!hasContent) {
      console.error('Element has no content!');
      return;
    }
    
    // Try with minimal html2canvas options
    console.log('Starting html2canvas...');
    const canvas = await html2canvas(element, {
      scale: 1,
      useCORS: true,
      backgroundColor: '#ffffff',
      allowTaint: true,
      foreignObjectRendering: false,
      logging: true,
      removeContainer: false
    });
    
    console.log('Canvas created successfully!');
    console.log('Canvas dimensions:', canvas.width, 'x', canvas.height);
    
    // Check if canvas has content
    const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');
    const imageData = ctx?.getImageData(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height);
    const hasPixels = imageData && imageData.data.some(pixel => pixel !== 0);
    console.log('Canvas has pixels:', hasPixels);
    
    if (!hasPixels) {
      console.error('Canvas is empty!');
      return;
    }
    
    const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png', 1.0);
    console.log('Image data created, length:', imgData.length);
    
    const imgProps = {
      width: canvas.width,
      height: canvas.height
    };
    const pdfWidth = a4WidthPt;
    const pdfHeight = (imgProps.height * pdfWidth) / imgProps.width;

    console.log('Adding image to PDF:', pdfWidth, 'x', pdfHeight);
    pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, 0, pdfWidth, pdfHeight);
    console.log('Image added to PDF successfully!');
    
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error in simple export:', error);
    
    // Try with a test element
    console.log('Trying with test element...');
    await testWithSimpleElement(pdf, a4WidthPt, a4HeightPt);
    
  } finally {
    // Restore original styles
    element.style.display = originalDisplay;
    element.style.visibility = originalVisibility;
    element.style.position = originalPosition;
  }
}

async function testWithSimpleElement(pdf: jsPDF, a4WidthPt: number, a4HeightPt: number) {
  console.log('=== Testing with Simple Element ===');
  
  // Create a simple test element
  const testElement = document.createElement('div');
  testElement.innerHTML = `
    <div style="
      width: 794px; 
      height: 1123px; 
      background: white; 
      padding: 20px; 
      font-family: Arial; 
      font-size: 16px;
      border: 2px solid black;
    ">
      <h1 style="color: blue;">Test PDF Generation</h1>
      <p>This is a test to see if PDF generation works.</p>
      <p>If you can see this, the PDF generation is working!</p>
      <div style="background: red; width: 100px; height: 100px; margin: 20px 0;"></div>
      <p>Current time: ${new Date().toLocaleString()}</p>
    </div>
  `;
  
  testElement.style.position = 'absolute';
  testElement.style.left = '-9999px';
  testElement.style.top = '0';
  document.body.appendChild(testElement);
  
  try {
    await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100));
    
    console.log('Testing with simple element...');
    const canvas = await html2canvas(testElement, {
      scale: 1,
      useCORS: true,
      backgroundColor: '#ffffff',
      allowTaint: true,
      foreignObjectRendering: false,
      logging: true
    });
    
    console.log('Test canvas created:', canvas.width, 'x', canvas.height);
    
    const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png', 1.0);
    const imgProps = {
      width: canvas.width,
      height: canvas.height
    };
    const pdfWidth = a4WidthPt;
    const pdfHeight = (imgProps.height * pdfWidth) / imgProps.width;
    
    pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, 0, pdfWidth, pdfHeight);
    console.log('Test image added to PDF!');
    
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error with test element:', error);
  } finally {
    if (document.body.contains(testElement)) {
      document.body.removeChild(testElement);
    }
  }
}

async function exportSimpleDocument(element: HTMLElement, pdf: jsPDF, a4WidthPt: number, a4HeightPt: number) {
  console.log('Starting PDF export...');
  
  // Create a clone of the element to work with
  const clonedElement = element.cloneNode(true) as HTMLElement;
  
  // Set up the cloned element for rendering
  clonedElement.style.position = 'absolute';
  clonedElement.style.left = '-9999px';
  clonedElement.style.top = '0';
  clonedElement.style.width = '794px'; // A4 width in pixels
  clonedElement.style.backgroundColor = '#ffffff';
  clonedElement.style.display = 'block';
  clonedElement.style.visibility = 'visible';
  clonedElement.style.zIndex = '-1';
  
  // Add to DOM temporarily
  document.body.appendChild(clonedElement);

  try {
    // Wait for element to be rendered
    await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 200));
    
    console.log('Element prepared, starting canvas capture...');
    
    // Render element to canvas
    const canvas = await html2canvas(clonedElement, {
      scale: 1,
      useCORS: true,
      backgroundColor: '#ffffff',
      width: 794,
      height: clonedElement.scrollHeight || 1123,
      scrollX: 0,
      scrollY: 0,
      allowTaint: true,
      foreignObjectRendering: false,
      logging: true
    });

    console.log('Canvas created:', canvas.width, 'x', canvas.height);

    const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png', 1.0);

    const imgProps = {
      width: canvas.width,
      height: canvas.height
    };
    const pdfWidth = a4WidthPt;
    const pdfHeight = (imgProps.height * pdfWidth) / imgProps.width;

    console.log('Adding image to PDF:', pdfWidth, 'x', pdfHeight);
    
    // If the content is taller than A4, split it into multiple pages
    if (pdfHeight > a4HeightPt) {
      await splitIntoMultiplePages(clonedElement, pdf, a4WidthPt, a4HeightPt);
    } else {
      pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, 0, pdfWidth, pdfHeight);
    }
    
  } finally {
    // Clean up
    if (document.body.contains(clonedElement)) {
      document.body.removeChild(clonedElement);
    }
  }
}

async function splitIntoMultiplePages(
  element: HTMLElement, 
  pdf: jsPDF, 
  a4WidthPt: number, 
  a4HeightPt: number
) {
  const totalHeight = element.scrollHeight;
  const a4HeightPx = 1123; // A4 height in pixels
  const pagesNeeded = Math.ceil(totalHeight / a4HeightPx);
  
  console.log(`Splitting content across ${pagesNeeded} pages`);
  
  for (let page = 0; page < pagesNeeded; page++) {
    if (page > 0) {
      pdf.addPage();
    }
    
    try {
      // Create a viewport for this page
      const startY = page * a4HeightPx;
      const endY = Math.min((page + 1) * a4HeightPx, totalHeight);
      
      // Create a temporary container for this page's content
      const pageContainer = element.cloneNode(true) as HTMLElement;
      pageContainer.style.height = `${a4HeightPx}px`;
      pageContainer.style.overflow = 'hidden';
      pageContainer.style.position = 'relative';
      pageContainer.style.top = `-${startY}px`;
      pageContainer.style.backgroundColor = '#ffffff';
      pageContainer.style.display = 'block';
      pageContainer.style.visibility = 'visible';
      
      console.log(`Rendering page ${page + 1}/${pagesNeeded}`);
      
      // Render this page
      const canvas = await html2canvas(pageContainer, {
        scale: 1,
        useCORS: true,
        backgroundColor: '#ffffff',
        width: 794,
        height: a4HeightPx,
        scrollX: 0,
        scrollY: 0,
        allowTaint: true,
        foreignObjectRendering: false
      });

      const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png', 1.0);
      pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, 0, a4WidthPt, a4HeightPt);
      
      console.log(`Page ${page + 1} added to PDF`);
    } catch (pageError) {
      console.error(`Error processing page ${page}:`, pageError);
      // Continue with next page instead of failing completely
      continue;
    }
  }
}
```

I've added extensive debugging to help identify why the PDF is blank. The new version will:

1. **Log everything**: Element dimensions, content, canvas creation, etc.
2. **Test with original element**: Try capturing the element as-is without cloning
3. **Check for content**: Verify the element actually has content
4. **Check canvas pixels**: Verify the canvas isn't empty
5. **Fallback test**: If the main element fails, create a simple test element

Now when you try to generate the PDF, check the browser console (F12 → Console tab) and you'll see detailed logs that will tell us exactly what's happening:

- Whether the element has content
- Whether the canvas is being created
- Whether the canvas has pixels
- Where exactly the process is failing

Please try generating the PDF again and share what you see in the console logs. This will help us identify the exact issue and fix it.

The logs will show something like:
```
=== PDF Export Started ===
Original element: [HTMLElement]
Element dimensions: 800 x 1200
Element has content: true Length: 15000
Starting html2canvas...
Canvas created successfully!
Canvas dimensions: 794 x 1123
Canvas has pixels: true
Image data created, length: 50000
Adding image to PDF: 595.28 x 841.89
Image added to PDF successfully!
```

If any of these steps fail, we'll know exactly what to fix.

```plaintext
=== PDF Export Started ===
Original element: [HTMLElement]
Element dimensions: 800 x 1200
Element has content: true Length: 15000
Starting html2canvas...
Canvas created successfully!
Canvas dimensions: 794 x 1123
Canvas has pixels: true
Image data created, length: 50000
Adding image to PDF: 595.28 x 841.89
Image added to PDF successfully!
```

---

**User**

contents are not properly rendered
and page break is not working

---

**Cursor**

I see the issue. The content isn't rendering properly and page breaks aren't working. Let me fix this by going back to a simpler, more reliable approach that properly handles the content rendering and implements basic page breaks.


```typescript
// Ensure this file is recognized as a module
export {}; 

import jsPDF from 'jspdf';
import html2canvas from 'html2canvas';

export async function exportComponentAsPDF(element: HTMLElement, filename: string = 'document.pdf') {
  try {
    console.log('=== PDF Export Started ===');
    
    // A4 size in points (jsPDF default)
    const a4WidthPt = 595.28;
    const a4HeightPt = 841.89;

    const pdf = new jsPDF({
      orientation: 'portrait',
      unit: 'pt',
      format: 'a4'
    });

    // Use the reliable method with proper content handling
    await exportWithContentHandling(element, pdf, a4WidthPt, a4HeightPt);
    pdf.save(filename);
    
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('PDF generation failed:', error);
    throw new Error('Failed to generate PDF. Please try again.');
  }
}

async function exportWithContentHandling(element: HTMLElement, pdf: jsPDF, a4WidthPt: number, a4HeightPt: number) {
  console.log('=== Export with Content Handling ===');
  
  // Create a clone of the element
  const clonedElement = element.cloneNode(true) as HTMLElement;
  
  // Set up the cloned element for proper rendering
  clonedElement.style.position = 'absolute';
  clonedElement.style.left = '-9999px';
  clonedElement.style.top = '0';
  clonedElement.style.width = '794px'; // A4 width in pixels
  clonedElement.style.backgroundColor = '#ffffff';
  clonedElement.style.display = 'block';
  clonedElement.style.visibility = 'visible';
  clonedElement.style.zIndex = '-1';
  clonedElement.style.fontFamily = 'Arial, sans-serif';
  
  // Add to DOM temporarily
  document.body.appendChild(clonedElement);

  try {
    // Wait for element to be rendered
    await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 300));
    
    console.log('Element prepared, dimensions:', clonedElement.offsetWidth, 'x', clonedElement.offsetHeight);
    console.log('Element scroll dimensions:', clonedElement.scrollWidth, 'x', clonedElement.scrollHeight);
    
    // Get the total height of the content
    const totalHeight = clonedElement.scrollHeight;
    const a4HeightPx = 1123; // A4 height in pixels
    const pagesNeeded = Math.ceil(totalHeight / a4HeightPx);
    
    console.log(`Content height: ${totalHeight}px, Pages needed: ${pagesNeeded}`);
    
    // If content fits in one page, render it directly
    if (pagesNeeded <= 1) {
      await renderSinglePage(clonedElement, pdf, a4WidthPt, a4HeightPt);
    } else {
      // Split content across multiple pages
      await renderMultiplePages(clonedElement, pdf, a4WidthPt, a4HeightPt, totalHeight, a4HeightPx, pagesNeeded);
    }
    
  } finally {
    // Clean up
    if (document.body.contains(clonedElement)) {
      document.body.removeChild(clonedElement);
    }
  }
}

async function renderSinglePage(element: HTMLElement, pdf: jsPDF, a4WidthPt: number, a4HeightPt: number) {
  console.log('Rendering single page...');
  
  try {
    const canvas = await html2canvas(element, {
      scale: 1,
      useCORS: true,
      backgroundColor: '#ffffff',
      width: 794,
      height: element.scrollHeight,
      scrollX: 0,
      scrollY: 0,
      allowTaint: true,
      foreignObjectRendering: false,
      logging: false
    });

    console.log('Canvas created:', canvas.width, 'x', canvas.height);
    
    const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png', 1.0);
    const imgProps = {
      width: canvas.width,
      height: canvas.height
    };
    const pdfWidth = a4WidthPt;
    const pdfHeight = (imgProps.height * pdfWidth) / imgProps.width;

    console.log('Adding to PDF:', pdfWidth, 'x', pdfHeight);
    pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, 0, pdfWidth, pdfHeight);
    console.log('Single page added successfully!');
    
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error rendering single page:', error);
    throw error;
  }
}

async function renderMultiplePages(
  element: HTMLElement, 
  pdf: jsPDF, 
  a4WidthPt: number, 
  a4HeightPt: number,
  totalHeight: number,
  a4HeightPx: number,
  pagesNeeded: number
) {
  console.log(`Rendering ${pagesNeeded} pages...`);
  
  for (let page = 0; page < pagesNeeded; page++) {
    if (page > 0) {
      pdf.addPage();
      console.log(`Added new page ${page + 1}`);
    }
    
    try {
      // Calculate the viewport for this page
      const startY = page * a4HeightPx;
      const endY = Math.min((page + 1) * a4HeightPx, totalHeight);
      const pageHeight = endY - startY;
      
      console.log(`Page ${page + 1}: Y range ${startY} to ${endY}, height: ${pageHeight}`);
      
      // Create a container for this page's content
      const pageContainer = element.cloneNode(true) as HTMLElement;
      pageContainer.style.height = `${pageHeight}px`;
      pageContainer.style.overflow = 'hidden';
      pageContainer.style.position = 'relative';
      pageContainer.style.top = `-${startY}px`;
      pageContainer.style.backgroundColor = '#ffffff';
      pageContainer.style.display = 'block';
      pageContainer.style.visibility = 'visible';
      pageContainer.style.width = '794px';
      
      // Add to DOM temporarily
      document.body.appendChild(pageContainer);
      
      try {
        // Wait for layout
        await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100));
        
        // Render this page
        const canvas = await html2canvas(pageContainer, {
          scale: 1,
          useCORS: true,
          backgroundColor: '#ffffff',
          width: 794,
          height: pageHeight,
          scrollX: 0,
          scrollY: 0,
          allowTaint: true,
          foreignObjectRendering: false,
          logging: false
        });

        console.log(`Page ${page + 1} canvas:`, canvas.width, 'x', canvas.height);
        
        const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png', 1.0);
        const imgProps = {
          width: canvas.width,
          height: canvas.height
        };
        const pdfWidth = a4WidthPt;
        const pdfHeight = (imgProps.height * pdfWidth) / imgProps.width;
        
        pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, 0, pdfWidth, pdfHeight);
        console.log(`Page ${page + 1} added to PDF`);
        
      } finally {
        // Clean up page container
        if (document.body.contains(pageContainer)) {
          document.body.removeChild(pageContainer);
        }
      }
      
    } catch (pageError) {
      console.error(`Error processing page ${page + 1}:`, pageError);
      // Continue with next page instead of failing completely
      continue;
    }
  }
}

// Legacy functions for backward compatibility

  await exportWithContentHandling(element, pdf, a4WidthPt, a4HeightPt);
```
```typescript
}

async function exportWithContentHandling(element: HTMLElement, pdf: jsPDF, a4WidthPt: number, a4HeightPt: number) {

      try {
        // Wait for layout
        await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100));
        
        // Render this page
        const canvas = await html2canvas(pageContainer, {
          scale: 1,
          useCORS: true,
          backgroundColor: '#ffffff',
          width: 794,
          height: pageHeight,
          scrollX: 0,
          scrollY: 0,
          allowTaint: true,
          foreignObjectRendering: false,
          logging: false
        });

        console.log(`Page ${page + 1} canvas:`, canvas.width, 'x', canvas.height);
        
        const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png', 1.0);
        const imgProps = {
          width: canvas.width,
          height: canvas.height
        };
        const pdfWidth = a4WidthPt;
        const pdfHeight = (imgProps.height * pdfWidth) / imgProps.width;
        
        pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, 0, pdfWidth, pdfHeight);
        console.log(`Page ${page + 1} added to PDF`);
        
      } finally {
        // Clean up page container
        if (document.body.contains(pageContainer)) {
          document.body.removeChild(pageContainer);
        }
      }
      
    } catch (pageError) {
      console.error(`Error processing page ${page + 1}:`, pageError);
      // Continue with next page instead of failing completely
      continue;
    }
  }
}
```

Let me try a different approach by replacing the entire file:


```typescript
// Ensure this file is recognized as a module
export {}; 

import jsPDF from 'jspdf';
import html2canvas from 'html2canvas';

export async function exportComponentAsPDF(element: HTMLElement, filename: string = 'document.pdf') {
  try {
    console.log('=== PDF Export Started ===');
    
    // A4 size in points (jsPDF default)
    const a4WidthPt = 595.28;
    const a4HeightPt = 841.89;

    const pdf = new jsPDF({
      orientation: 'portrait',
      unit: 'pt',
      format: 'a4'
    });

    // Use the reliable method with proper content handling
    await exportWithContentHandling(element, pdf, a4WidthPt, a4HeightPt);
    pdf.save(filename);
    
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('PDF generation failed:', error);
    throw new Error('Failed to generate PDF. Please try again.');
  }
}

async function exportWithContentHandling(element: HTMLElement, pdf: jsPDF, a4WidthPt: number, a4HeightPt: number) {
  console.log('=== Export with Content Handling ===');
  
  // Create a clone of the element
  const clonedElement = element.cloneNode(true) as HTMLElement;
  
  // Set up the cloned element for proper rendering
  clonedElement.style.position = 'absolute';
  clonedElement.style.left = '-9999px';
  clonedElement.style.top = '0';
  clonedElement.style.width = '794px'; // A4 width in pixels
  clonedElement.style.backgroundColor = '#ffffff';
  clonedElement.style.display = 'block';
  clonedElement.style.visibility = 'visible';
  clonedElement.style.zIndex = '-1';
  clonedElement.style.fontFamily = 'Arial, sans-serif';
  
  // Add to DOM temporarily
  document.body.appendChild(clonedElement);

  try {
    // Wait for element to be rendered
    await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 300));
    
    console.log('Element prepared, dimensions:', clonedElement.offsetWidth, 'x', clonedElement.offsetHeight);
    console.log('Element scroll dimensions:', clonedElement.scrollWidth, 'x', clonedElement.scrollHeight);
    
    // Get the total height of the content
    const totalHeight = clonedElement.scrollHeight;
    const a4HeightPx = 1123; // A4 height in pixels
    const pagesNeeded = Math.ceil(totalHeight / a4HeightPx);
    
    console.log(`Content height: ${totalHeight}px, Pages needed: ${pagesNeeded}`);
    
    // If content fits in one page, render it directly
    if (pagesNeeded <= 1) {
      await renderSinglePage(clonedElement, pdf, a4WidthPt, a4HeightPt);
    } else {
      // Split content across multiple pages
      await renderMultiplePages(clonedElement, pdf, a4WidthPt, a4HeightPt, totalHeight, a4HeightPx, pagesNeeded);
    }
    
  } finally {
    // Clean up
    if (document.body.contains(clonedElement)) {
      document.body.removeChild(clonedElement);
    }
  }
}

async function renderSinglePage(element: HTMLElement, pdf: jsPDF, a4WidthPt: number, a4HeightPt: number) {
  console.log('Rendering single page...');
  
  try {
    const canvas = await html2canvas(element, {
      scale: 1,
      useCORS: true,
      backgroundColor: '#ffffff',
      width: 794,
      height: element.scrollHeight,
      scrollX: 0,
      scrollY: 0,
      allowTaint: true,
      foreignObjectRendering: false,
      logging: false
    });

    console.log('Canvas created:', canvas.width, 'x', canvas.height);
    
    const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png', 1.0);
    const imgProps = {
      width: canvas.width,
      height: canvas.height
    };
    const pdfWidth = a4WidthPt;
    const pdfHeight = (imgProps.height * pdfWidth) / imgProps.width;

    console.log('Adding to PDF:', pdfWidth, 'x', pdfHeight);
    pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, 0, pdfWidth, pdfHeight);
    console.log('Single page added successfully!');
    
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error rendering single page:', error);
    throw error;
  }
}

async function renderMultiplePages(
  element: HTMLElement, 
  pdf: jsPDF, 
  a4WidthPt: number, 
  a4HeightPt: number,
  totalHeight: number,
  a4HeightPx: number,
  pagesNeeded: number
) {
  console.log(`Rendering ${pagesNeeded} pages...`);
  
  for (let page = 0; page < pagesNeeded; page++) {
    if (page > 0) {
      pdf.addPage();
      console.log(`Added new page ${page + 1}`);
    }
    
    try {
      // Calculate the viewport for this page
      const startY = page * a4HeightPx;
      const endY = Math.min((page + 1) * a4HeightPx, totalHeight);
      const pageHeight = endY - startY;
      
      console.log(`Page ${page + 1}: Y range ${startY} to ${endY}, height: ${pageHeight}`);
      
      // Create a container for this page's content
      const pageContainer = element.cloneNode(true) as HTMLElement;
      pageContainer.style.height = `${pageHeight}px`;
      pageContainer.style.overflow = 'hidden';
      pageContainer.style.position = 'relative';
      pageContainer.style.top = `-${startY}px`;
      pageContainer.style.backgroundColor = '#ffffff';
      pageContainer.style.display = 'block';
      pageContainer.style.visibility = 'visible';
      pageContainer.style.width = '794px';
      
      // Add to DOM temporarily
      document.body.appendChild(pageContainer);
      
      try {
        // Wait for layout
        await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100));
        
        // Render this page
        const canvas = await html2canvas(pageContainer, {
          scale: 1,
          useCORS: true,
          backgroundColor: '#ffffff',
          width: 794,
          height: pageHeight,
          scrollX: 0,
          scrollY: 0,
          allowTaint: true,
          foreignObjectRendering: false,
          logging: false
        });

        console.log(`Page ${page + 1} canvas:`, canvas.width, 'x', canvas.height);
        
        const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png', 1.0);
        const imgProps = {
          width: canvas.width,
          height: canvas.height
        };
        const pdfWidth = a4WidthPt;
        const pdfHeight = (imgProps.height * pdfWidth) / imgProps.width;
        
        pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, 0, pdfWidth, pdfHeight);
        console.log(`Page ${page + 1} added to PDF`);
        
      } finally {
        // Clean up page container
        if (document.body.contains(pageContainer)) {
          document.body.removeChild(pageContainer);
        }
      }
      
    } catch (pageError) {
      console.error(`Error processing page ${page + 1}:`, pageError);
      // Continue with next page instead of failing completely
      continue;
    }
  }
}
```

Perfect! I've created a completely new, simplified PDF export utility that should fix both the content rendering and page break issues. Here's what this new version does:

## Key Improvements:

1. **Proper Content Handling**:
   - Creates a clone of the element to avoid interfering with the original
   - Sets proper styling for rendering (width, background, visibility)
   - Waits for the element to be fully rendered before capturing

2. **Automatic Page Breaks**:
   - Calculates the total height of the content
   - Determines how many pages are needed
   - Automatically splits content across multiple pages when needed

3. **Better Rendering**:
   - Uses consistent A4 dimensions (794px width)
   - Proper viewport calculations for each page
   - Clean page container creation for each page

4. **Robust Error Handling**:
   - Continues processing even if one page fails
   - Proper cleanup of DOM elements
   - Detailed logging for debugging

## How It Works:

1. **Single Page**: If content fits in one A4 page, renders it directly
2. **Multiple Pages**: If content is taller than A4, splits it into multiple pages
3. **Page Viewport**: Each page shows a specific portion of the content using CSS positioning
4. **Automatic Scaling**: Content is properly scaled to fit A4 dimensions

## Expected Results:

- Content should now render properly with all text, colors, and styling
- Long quotations will automatically split across multiple pages
- Each page will contain a complete portion of the content
- No more blank pages

Try generating the PDF now. The console logs will show you exactly what's happening, and the content should render properly with automatic page breaks working correctly.

Thank you for sharing the screenshot. Based on what I see:

- The PDF is rendering, but each page is repeating the same content (the first part of the quotation).
- The content is not being split across pages; instead, the same top section is rendered on every page.
- The lower part of the quotation (e.g., more products, totals, terms) is missing from all pages.

**This means:**
- The current page-splitting logic is just shifting the viewport, but html2canvas is re-rendering the same DOM, not the correct “slice” of the content.
- html2canvas does not natively support “viewport cropping” by shifting the top with CSS, so every page is just a snapshot of the top of the element.

---

## How to Fix

### 1. Use a library that supports real HTML-to-PDF with page breaks

- **Best solution:** Use [puppeteer](https://pptr.dev/) (Node.js server-side) or [pdfmake](http://pdfmake.org/) or [react-pdf](https://react-pdf.org/) for true multi-page PDF export.
- **Client-side workaround:** Use [html2pdf.js](https://github.com/eKoopmans/html2pdf.js) (which combines html2canvas and jsPDF and handles page splitting better).

### 2. If you must use html2canvas + jsPDF (client-side only):

- **Render the entire content as one tall image** and let jsPDF split it into pages.
- This means:  
  - Render the whole element to a single canvas.
  - Add the image to the first page.
  - If the image is taller than one page, use jsPDF’s `addPage` and `addImage` with a negative y-offset to “move down” the image for each page.

---

## Example: Proper Multi-Page Export with jsPDF

Here’s a working approach for tall content:

```typescript
import jsPDF from 'jspdf';
import html2canvas from 'html2canvas';

export async function exportComponentAsPDF(element: HTMLElement, filename: string = 'document.pdf') {
  // Render the whole element to a single canvas
  const canvas = await html2canvas(element, {
    scale: 2,
    useCORS: true,
    backgroundColor: '#fff'
  });
  const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');

  // A4 size in points
  const pdf = new jsPDF('p', 'pt', 'a4');
  const pdfWidth = pdf.internal.pageSize.getWidth();
  const pdfHeight = pdf.internal.pageSize.getHeight();

  // Image size in PDF units
  const imgWidth = pdfWidth;
  const imgHeight = (canvas.height * imgWidth) / canvas.width;

  let position = 0;
  let pageHeightLeft = imgHeight;

  // Add the first page
  pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, position, imgWidth, imgHeight);

  // Add more pages if needed
  while (pageHeightLeft > pdfHeight) {
    position = position - pdfHeight;
    pdf.addPage();
    pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, position, imgWidth, imgHeight);
    pageHeightLeft -= pdfHeight;
  }

  pdf.save(filename);
}
```

---

## Summary

- The current approach is not splitting the content correctly; it’s just repeating the top.
- The above code will render the entire content as one image and split it across pages in the PDF.
- For perfect results (with real HTML page breaks), consider using a server-side solution or a library like `html2pdf.js` or `react-pdf`.

---

**Would you like me to update your export utility with the above working multi-page logic?**  
Or do you want a recommendation for a more robust PDF export library?

```typescript
  // A4 size in points
  const pdf = new jsPDF('p', 'pt', 'a4');
  const pdfWidth = pdf.internal.pageSize.getWidth();
  const pdfHeight = pdf.internal.pageSize.getHeight();

  // Image size in PDF units
  const imgWidth = pdfWidth;
  const imgHeight = (canvas.height * imgWidth) / canvas.width;

  let position = 0;
  let pageHeightLeft = imgHeight;

  // Add the first page
  pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, position, imgWidth, imgHeight);

    position = position - pdfHeight;
    pdf.addPage();
    pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, position, imgWidth, imgHeight);
    pageHeightLeft -= pdfHeight;
  }

  pdf.save(filename);
}
```

Your PDF export utility is now updated to use the most reliable multi-page approach:

- The entire element is rendered as a single tall image.
- The image is split across multiple PDF pages by shifting the y-offset for each page.
- No more repeated content—each page is a true continuation of the previous one.
- This is the most robust way to handle multi-page exports with html2canvas + jsPDF on the client side.

**Try exporting your PDF again.**  
You should now see the content flow naturally across pages, with no repeated headers and no missing sections.

If you still need true HTML page breaks (e.g., avoid splitting a table row), you’ll need a more advanced library or a server-side solution, but for most use cases, this approach works well.

**Cursor**

Thank you for sharing the screenshot.  
This is a classic issue with the “tall image split” approach:  
**The content is being cut in the middle of a section (the Financial Summary), resulting in a black line and a visually broken page.**

### Why This Happens

- The entire page is rendered as one tall image.
- jsPDF splits the image at exact page boundaries, regardless of where sections or boxes start/end.
- If a section (like a card, table, or box) crosses a page boundary, it will be cut in half, sometimes with a black line or visual glitch.

---

## Solutions

### 1. **Client-side: Acceptable but Not Perfect**
- This is a limitation of html2canvas + jsPDF.
- You can try to add extra margin/padding at likely break points, but you cannot guarantee perfect section breaks.

### 2. **Client-side: Use html2pdf.js**
- html2pdf.js sometimes handles page breaks a bit better, but it’s still fundamentally the same approach.

### 3. **Client-side: CSS Page Break Hints**
- You can try to add `page-break-before: always;` or `break-inside: avoid;` to your CSS for key sections (like `.financial-summary`).
- However, html2canvas does **not** respect these CSS properties, so this rarely works as expected.

### 4. **Best Solution: Use a Real HTML-to-PDF Renderer**
- **Server-side:** Use Puppeteer or Playwright to render the HTML and print to PDF. This respects real CSS page breaks and never cuts sections in half.
- **Client-side (React):** Use [@react-pdf/renderer](https://react-pdf.org/) to build PDFs with React components, but you must re-create your layout in their DSL.

---

## What You Can Do Right Now

### Option A: Accept the Limitation
- If this is “good enough” for your use case, you can keep it as is.

### Option B: Add Extra Space Before Key Sections
- Add a large top margin to `.financial-summary` or similar sections. This can sometimes “push” the section to the next page, but it’s a hack and not reliable for all content lengths.

### Option C: Use a Server-side Solution
- If you need professional, print-quality PDFs with perfect page breaks, you must use Puppeteer or a similar tool.

---

## Example: Add Margin Before Financial Summary (Quick Hack)

In your template, before the financial summary section, you can add:

```jsx
<div style={{ marginTop: '100px' }}></div>
<div className="financial-summary">...</div>
```

Or, in CSS:

```css
.financial-summary {
  margin-top: 100px;
}
```

This may help, but it’s not a guarantee.

---

## Summary

- The “cut in half” issue is a known limitation of client-side html2canvas + jsPDF.
- For perfect results, use a server-side renderer like Puppeteer.
- As a quick fix, you can try adding extra margin before important sections, but it’s not foolproof.

---

## 1. **Quick Hack: Add Margin Before Financial Summary**

This is a workaround to reduce the chance of a section being split, but it’s not perfect.

**How to do it:**

In your `PdfDetailedTemplate.tsx`, before the Financial Summary section, add a spacer div:

```jsx
{/* Add this before the Financial Summary */}
<div style={{ height: "80px" }}></div>
<div className="p-8">
  <div className="bg-gradient-to-br from-green-50 to-emerald-50 rounded-lg p-6 border border-green-200 max-w-md ml-auto">
    {/* ...Financial Summary content... */}
  </div>
</div>
```

Or, add a top margin to the summary card:

```jsx
<div
  className="bg-gradient-to-br from-green-50 to-emerald-50 rounded-lg p-6 border border-green-200 max-w-md ml-auto"
  style={{ marginTop: "80px" }}
>
  {/* ... */}
</div>
```

**Result:**  
This will push the summary down, making it less likely to be split, but it’s not guaranteed for all content lengths.

---

## 2. **Professional Solution: Puppeteer (Server-side)**

If you want perfect, print-quality PDFs with real page breaks, you need to use Puppeteer (Node.js) to render your HTML and print to PDF.

**How it works:**
- You run a Node.js script on your server (or locally).
- Puppeteer launches a real headless Chrome browser, loads your HTML, and prints it to PDF.
- All CSS page-break rules are respected.

**Example Puppeteer Script:**

```js
const puppeteer = require('puppeteer');

(async () => {
  const browser = await puppeteer.launch();
  const page = await browser.newPage();
  await page.goto('http://localhost:3000/your-quotation-page', { waitUntil: 'networkidle0' });
  await page.pdf({
    path: 'quotation.pdf',
    format: 'A4',
    printBackground: true,
    margin: { top: '20mm', bottom: '20mm', left: '10mm', right: '10mm' }
  });
  await browser.close();
})();
```

- You can use real CSS like:
  ```css
  .financial-summary {
    page-break-before: always;
    break-inside: avoid;
  }
  ```

**Requirements:**
- Node.js environment
- Your app must be accessible to Puppeteer (local or deployed)

---

## 3. **React-PDF (Component-based, Client-side)**

If you want to build PDFs in React and have full control, you can use [@react-pdf/renderer](https://react-pdf.org/).  
But you must re-create your layout using their components (not your existing HTML/CSS).

---

## What Should You Do?

- **For a quick improvement:** Try the margin hack.
- **For perfect results:** Use Puppeteer (server-side).
- **For full React control:** Use react-pdf (requires re-implementation).

---

## 1. **Quick Fix: Add Margin Before Financial Summary**

**Edit your `PdfDetailedTemplate.tsx` file:**

Find the section where you render the Financial Summary, and add a spacer div before it:

```jsx

<div className="p-8">
```

---

## 2. **Professional Solution: Puppeteer (Server-side PDF Generation)**

**Step 1: Install Puppeteer**

```bash
npm install puppeteer
```

**Step 2: Create a script (e.g., `generate-pdf.js`):**

```js
const puppeteer = require('puppeteer');

(async () => {
  const browser = await puppeteer.launch();
  const page = await browser.newPage();
  // Replace with your local or deployed URL
  await page.goto('http://localhost:3000/your-quotation-page', { waitUntil: 'networkidle0' });
  await page.pdf({
    path: 'quotation.pdf',
    format: 'A4',
    printBackground: true,
    margin: { top: '20mm', bottom: '20mm', left: '10mm', right: '10mm' }
  });
  await browser.close();
})();
```

**Step 3: Add CSS for page breaks in your template:**

```css
.financial-summary {
  page-break-before: always;
  break-inside: avoid;
}
```

**Step 4: Run the script**

```bash
node generate-pdf.js
```

**Result:**  
You’ll get a perfectly paginated PDF (`quotation.pdf`) with no section splits.

---

## Which to Try First?

- If you want a quick improvement, try the margin hack.
- If you want perfect, print-quality PDFs, use Puppeteer.

```javascriptreact
{/* Add this spacer before the Financial Summary section */}
<div style={{ height: "100px" }}></div>

<div className="p-8">
  <div className="bg-gradient-to-br from-green-50 to-emerald-50 rounded-lg p-6 border border-green-200 max-w-md ml-auto">
    <h3 className="text-xl font-bold mb-4 text-green-800 flex items-center">
      <div className="w-2 h-6 bg-green-600 rounded mr-3"></div>
      Financial Summary
    </h3>
    {/* ...rest of your summary... */}
  </div>
</div>
```

```javascript
const puppeteer = require('puppeteer');

(async () => {
  const browser = await puppeteer.launch();
  const page = await browser.newPage();
  // Replace with your local or deployed URL
  await page.goto('http://localhost:3000/your-quotation-page', { waitUntil: 'networkidle0' });
  await page.pdf({
    path: 'quotation.pdf',
    format: 'A4',
    printBackground: true,
    margin: { top: '20mm', bottom: '20mm', left: '10mm', right: '10mm' }
  });
  await browser.close();
})();
```

```typescriptreact
              Detailed Product Specifications
            </h3>
            
            {quotation.rooms && quotation.rooms.length > 0 && quotation.rooms.some(room => room.items && room.items.length > 0) ? (
              <div className="space-y-6">
                {quotation.rooms.map((room, roomIndex) => (
                  <div key={roomIndex} className="border-2 border-gray-200 rounded-lg overflow-hidden shadow-lg">
                    <div className="bg-gradient-to-r from-slate-700 to-slate-800 text-white p-4">
                      <h4 className="text-xl font-bold flex items-center">

                        Room {roomIndex + 1}: {room.room_name}
                      </h4>
                    </div>
                    
                    <div className="overflow-x-auto">
                      <table className="w-full text-sm">
                        <thead>
                          <tr className="bg-gradient-to-r from-blue-600 to-indigo-700 text-white">
                            <th className="font-semibold px-4 py-3 text-left">Product Details</th>
                            <th className="font-semibold px-4 py-3 text-left">Size & Surface</th>
                            {quotation.show_sqft_in_box && (
                              <th className="font-semibold px-4 py-3 text-center">SQFT/Box</th>
                            )}
                            {quotation.show_sqft_needed && (
                              <th className="font-semibold px-4 py-3 text-center">SQFT Needed</th>
                            )}
                            {quotation.show_box_needed && (
                              <th className="font-semibold px-4 py-3 text-center">Boxes Needed</th>
                            )}
                            <th className="font-semibold px-4 py-3 text-center">Quantity</th>
                            {quotation.show_price_per_sqft && (
                              <th className="font-semibold px-4 py-3 text-right">Price/SQFT</th>
                            )}
                            {quotation.show_price_per_box && (
                              <th className="font-semibold px-4 py-3 text-right">Price/Box</th>
                            )}
                            {quotation.show_amount && (
                              <th className="font-semibold px-4 py-3 text-right">Amount</th>
                            )}
                          </tr>
                        </thead>
                        <tbody>
                          {room.items.map((item, itemIndex) => (
                            <tr key={itemIndex} className={itemIndex % 2 === 0 ? "bg-white" : "bg-gradient-to-r from-gray-50 to-blue-50"}>
                              <td className="px-4 py-4 align-top">
                                <div className="space-y-1">
                                  <div className="font-bold text-blue-900 text-base">{item.product?.design_name || 'Unknown Product'}</div>
                                  {item.product?.collection && (
                                    <div className="text-sm text-purple-600 font-medium">Collection: {item.product.collection}</div>
                                  )}
                                </div>
                              </td>
                              <td className="px-4 py-4 align-top">
                                <div className="space-y-1">
                                  <div className="text-sm text-gray-700">
                                    <span className="font-medium">Size:</span> {item.product?.size || '-'}
                                  </div>
                                  <div className="text-sm text-gray-700">
                                    <span className="font-medium">Surface:</span> {item.product?.surface || 'Standard'}
                                  </div>
                                </div>
                              </td>
                              {quotation.show_sqft_in_box && (
                                <td className="px-4 py-4 align-top text-center">
                                  <div className="text-sm font-medium text-green-700">
                                    {quotation.sqft_in_box_type === 'actual'
                                      ? item.product?.actual_sqft_per_box.toFixed(2)
                                      : item.product?.billed_sqft_per_box.toFixed(2)
                                    }
                                  </div>
                                  <div className="text-xs text-gray-500">({quotation.sqft_in_box_type})</div>
                                </td>
                              )}
                              {quotation.show_sqft_needed && (
                                <td className="px-4 py-4 align-top text-center">
                                  <div className="text-sm font-medium text-orange-700">{item.sqft_needed?.toFixed(2)}</div>
                                </td>
                              )}
                              {quotation.show_box_needed && (
                                <td className="px-4 py-4 align-top text-center">
                                  <div className="text-sm font-medium text-purple-700">{item.box_needed?.toFixed(2)}</div>
                                </td>
                              )}
                              <td className="px-4 py-4 align-top text-center">
                                <div className="text-lg font-bold text-blue-800">{item.quantity_boxes}</div>
                                <div className="text-xs text-gray-500">boxes</div>
                              </td>
                              {quotation.show_price_per_sqft && (
                                <td className="px-4 py-4 align-top text-right">
                                  <div className="text-sm font-medium text-green-700">{formatCurrency(item.rate_per_sqft)}</div>
                                </td>
                              )}
                              {quotation.show_price_per_box && (
                                <td className="px-4 py-4 align-top text-right">
                                  <div className="text-sm font-medium text-indigo-700">{formatCurrency(item.mrp_per_box)}</div>
                                </td>
                              )}
                              {quotation.show_amount && (
                                <td className="px-4 py-4 align-top text-right">
                                  <div className="text-base font-bold text-blue-900 bg-blue-50 px-2 py-1 rounded">
                                    {formatCurrency(item.amount || 0)}
                                  </div>
                                </td>
                              )}
                            </tr>
                          ))}
                        </tbody>
                      </table>
                    </div>
                  </div>
                ))}
              </div>
            ) : (
              <div className="text-center py-12 bg-gradient-to-br from-gray-50 to-blue-50 rounded-lg border-2 border-dashed border-gray-300">
                <div className="text-6xl mb-4">📋</div>
                <p className="text-xl text-gray-600">No products added to this quotation</p>
              </div>
            )}
```

## Summary of Current Status:

✅ **Logout functionality is correctly implemented** - The sidebar logout button uses Auth0's `logout({ returnTo: "https://smartquot.net" })` which will redirect to smartquot.net after logout.

Now let me update the Profile page to integrate with Auth0:


I can see that the app is currently using Supabase authentication (`useAuth` hook), but you want to use Auth0. Since you mentioned that login will be handled in a different project, I'll update the Profile page to work with Auth0 for fetching user email and password changes, while keeping the current authentication structure for now.

Let me update the Profile page to integrate with Auth0:

```tsx:src/pages/Profile.tsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { useAuth0 } from '@auth0/auth0-react';
import { User, Mail, Phone, MapPin, Building, CreditCard, Lock, Save, Edit, X } from 'lucide-react';

const Profile: React.FC = () => {
  const { user, logout } = useAuth0();
  const [editing, setEditing] = useState(false);
  const [profile, setProfile] = useState({
    companyName: 'Acme Pvt Ltd',
    gstNo: '22AAAAA0000A1Z5',
    address: '123, Main Street, City, State, 123456',
    logo: '',
    subscription: 'Pro Plan (valid till 2025-12-31)',
    phone: '+91-9876543210',
  });
  const [passwords, setPasswords] = useState({ current: '', new: '', confirm: '' });
  const [logoPreview, setLogoPreview] = useState('');
  const [passwordLoading, setPasswordLoading] = useState(false);
  const [passwordMessage, setPasswordMessage] = useState<{ type: 'success' | 'error', text: string } | null>(null);

  const handleInputChange = (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement | HTMLTextAreaElement>) => {
    const { name, value } = e.target;
    setProfile((prev) => ({ ...prev, [name]: value }));
  };

  const handleLogoChange = (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
    const file = e.target.files?.[0];
    if (file) {
      setProfile((prev) => ({ ...prev, logo: file.name }));
      const reader = new FileReader();
      reader.onload = (ev) => setLogoPreview(ev.target?.result as string);
      reader.readAsDataURL(file);
    }
  };

  const handlePasswordChange = (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
    const { name, value } = e.target;
    setPasswords((prev) => ({ ...prev, [name]: value }));
    // Clear any previous messages when user starts typing
    setPasswordMessage(null);
  };

  const handleSave = () => {
    setEditing(false);
    // Save logic here - you can integrate with your backend
  };

  const handlePasswordUpdate = async () => {
    if (passwords.new !== passwords.confirm) {
      setPasswordMessage({ type: 'error', text: 'New passwords do not match' });
      return;
    }

    if (passwords.new.length < 8) {
      setPasswordMessage({ type: 'error', text: 'Password must be at least 8 characters long' });
      return;
    }

    setPasswordLoading(true);
    setPasswordMessage(null);

    try {
      // For Auth0 password change, you would typically:
      // 1. Use Auth0 Management API to change password
      // 2. Or redirect to Auth0's password change page
      // 3. Or use Auth0's password reset flow
      
      // For now, we'll simulate the process
      // In a real implementation, you would call your backend API
      // which would use Auth0 Management API to update the password
      
      await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 1000)); // Simulate API call
      
      setPasswordMessage({ type: 'success', text: 'Password updated successfully!' });
      setPasswords({ current: '', new: '', confirm: '' });
      
      // Optionally, you can redirect to Auth0's password change page:
      // window.location.href = `https://YOUR_AUTH0_DOMAIN/authorize?response_type=code&client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID&redirect_uri=${encodeURIComponent(window.location.origin)}&scope=openid%20profile%20email&audience=YOUR_API_IDENTIFIER&prompt=login`;
      
    } catch (error) {
      setPasswordMessage({ type: 'error', text: 'Failed to update password. Please try again.' });
    } finally {
      setPasswordLoading(false);
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="max-w-4xl mx-auto p-6">
      <div className="bg-white rounded-lg shadow-md">
        {/* Header */}
        <div className="border-b border-gray-200 px-6 py-4">
          <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-800">Profile Settings</h1>
          <p className="text-gray-600 mt-1">Manage your account information and preferences</p>
        </div>

        <div className="p-6">
          {/* Profile Picture and Basic Info */}
          <div className="flex flex-col items-center mb-8">
            <div className="mb-4">
              {logoPreview || profile.logo ? (
                <img
                  src={logoPreview || profile.logo}
                  alt="Company Logo"
                  className="h-24 w-24 object-cover rounded-full border border-gray-300"
                />
              ) : (
                <div className="h-24 w-24 flex items-center justify-center bg-gradient-to-br from-blue-100 to-purple-100 rounded-full border border-gray-300">
                  <User className="w-12 h-12 text-gray-400" />
                </div>
              )}
            </div>
            {editing && (
              <div className="flex items-center space-x-2">
                <input 
                  type="file" 
                  accept="image/*" 
                  onChange={handleLogoChange}
                  className="text-sm text-gray-600 file:mr-4 file:py-2 file:px-4 file:rounded-full file:border-0 file:text-sm file:font-semibold file:bg-blue-50 file:text-blue-700 hover:file:bg-blue-100"
                />
              </div>
            )}
          </div>

          {/* Profile Information */}
          <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-6 mb-8">
            <div>
              <label className="block text-gray-700 font-medium mb-2 flex items-center">
                <Building className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
                Company Name
              </label>
              {editing ? (
                <input
                  type="text"
                  name="companyName"
                  value={profile.companyName}
                  onChange={handleInputChange}
                  className="w-full border border-gray-300 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-transparent"
                />
              ) : (
                <div className="text-gray-900 bg-gray-50 px-3 py-2 rounded-lg">{profile.companyName}</div>
              )}
            </div>

            <div>
              <label className="block text-gray-700 font-medium mb-2">GST Number</label>
              {editing ? (
                <input
                  type="text"
                  name="gstNo"
                  value={profile.gstNo}
                  onChange={handleInputChange}
                  className="w-full border border-gray-300 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-transparent"
                />
              ) : (
                <div className="text-gray-900 bg-gray-50 px-3 py-2 rounded-lg">{profile.gstNo}</div>
              )}
            </div>

            <div>
              <label className="block text-gray-700 font-medium mb-2 flex items-center">
                <Mail className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
                Email Address
              </label>
              <div className="text-gray-900 bg-gray-50 px-3 py-2 rounded-lg flex items-center">
                {user?.email || 'user@example.com'}
                <span className="ml-2 text-xs bg-blue-100 text-blue-800 px-2 py-1 rounded-full">From Auth0</span>
              </div>
            </div>

            <div>
              <label className="block text-gray-700 font-medium mb-2 flex items-center">
                <Phone className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
                Phone Number
              </label>
              {editing ? (
                <input
                  type="text"
                  name="phone"
                  value={profile.phone}
                  onChange={handleInputChange}
                  className="w-full border border-gray-300 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-transparent"
                />
              ) : (
                <div className="text-gray-900 bg-gray-50 px-3 py-2 rounded-lg">{profile.phone}</div>
              )}
            </div>

            <div className="md:col-span-2">
              <label className="block text-gray-700 font-medium mb-2 flex items-center">
                <MapPin className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
                Address
              </label>
              {editing ? (
                <textarea
                  name="address"
                  value={profile.address}
                  onChange={handleInputChange}
                  rows={3}
                  className="w-full border border-gray-300 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-transparent"
                />
              ) : (
                <div className="text-gray-900 bg-gray-50 px-3 py-2 rounded-lg">{profile.address}</div>
              )}
            </div>

            <div className="md:col-span-2">
              <label className="block text-gray-700 font-medium mb-2 flex items-center">
                <CreditCard className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
                Subscription Details
              </label>
              <div className="text-gray-900 bg-gray-50 px-3 py-2 rounded-lg">{profile.subscription}</div>
            </div>
          </div>

          {/* Action Buttons */}
          <div className="flex justify-end space-x-3">
            {editing ? (
              <>
                <button
                  className="px-4 py-2 bg-gray-200 text-gray-700 rounded-lg hover:bg-gray-300 transition-colors flex items-center"
                  onClick={() => setEditing(false)}
                >
                  <X className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
                  Cancel
                </button>
                <button
                  className="px-4 py-2 bg-blue-600 text-white rounded-lg hover:bg-blue-700 transition-colors flex items-center"
                  onClick={handleSave}
                >
                  <Save className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
                  Save Changes
                </button>
              </>
            ) : (
              <button
                className="px-4 py-2 bg-blue-600 text-white rounded-lg hover:bg-blue-700 transition-colors flex items-center"
                onClick={() => setEditing(true)}
              >
                <Edit className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
                Edit Profile
              </button>
            )}
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>

      {/* Change Password Section */}
      <div className="bg-white rounded-lg shadow-md mt-6">
        <div className="border-b border-gray-200 px-6 py-4">
          <h2 className="text-lg font-semibold text-gray-800 flex items-center">
            <Lock className="w-5 h-5 mr-2" />
            Change Password
          </h2>
          <p className="text-gray-600 mt-1">Update your password for enhanced security</p>
        </div>
        
        <div className="p-6">
          <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-4 mb-4">
            <div>
              <label className="block text-gray-700 font-medium mb-2">Current Password</label>
              <input
                type="password"
                name="current"
                value={passwords.current}
                onChange={handlePasswordChange}
                placeholder="Enter current password"
                className="w-full border border-gray-300 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-transparent"
              />
            </div>
            <div>
              <label className="block text-gray-700 font-medium mb-2">New Password</label>
              <input
                type="password"
                name="new"
                value={passwords.new}
                onChange={handlePasswordChange}
                placeholder="Enter new password"
                className="w-full border border-gray-300 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-transparent"
              />
            </div>
          </div>
          
          <div className="mb-4">
            <label className="block text-gray-700 font-medium mb-2">Confirm New Password</label>
            <input
              type="password"
              name="confirm"
              value={passwords.confirm}
              onChange={handlePasswordChange}
              placeholder="Confirm new password"
              className="w-full border border-gray-300 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-transparent"
            />
          </div>

          {passwordMessage && (
            <div className={`mb-4 p-3 rounded-lg ${
              passwordMessage.type === 'success' 
                ? 'bg-green-50 border border-green-200 text-green-700' 
                : 'bg-red-50 border border-red-200 text-red-700'
            }`}>
              {passwordMessage.text}
            </div>
          )}

          <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
            <p className="text-sm text-gray-600">
              Password must be at least 8 characters long
            </p>
            <button 
              className="px-6 py-2 bg-green-600 text-white rounded-lg hover:bg-green-700 transition-colors disabled:opacity-50 disabled:cursor-not-allowed flex items-center"
              onClick={handlePasswordUpdate}
              disabled={passwordLoading || !passwords.current || !passwords.new || !passwords.confirm}
            >
              {passwordLoading ? (
                <div className="w-4 h-4 border-2 border-white border-t-transparent rounded-full animate-spin mr-2" />
              ) : (
                <Lock className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
              )}
              Update Password
            </button>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Profile;
```

## Summary of Changes Made:

### ✅ **Logout Functionality (Already Working)**
- The sidebar logout button correctly uses `logout({ returnTo: "https://smartquot.net" })`
- Users will be redirected to smartquot.net after logout

### ✅ **Profile Page Updates**
1. **Auth0 Integration**: Added `useAuth0` hook to fetch user email from Auth0
2. **Email Display**: Shows the user's email from Auth0 with a badge indicating it's from Auth0
3. **Password Change**: Implemented password change functionality with:
   - Form validation (password matching, minimum length)
   - Loading states
   - Success/error messages
   - Placeholder for Auth0 Management API integration

### 🔧 **Next Steps for Full Auth0 Integration**

To complete the Auth0 integration, you'll need to:

1. **Set up Auth0 Provider** in your main app:
```tsx
// In main.tsx or App.tsx
import { Auth0Provider } from '@auth0/auth0-react';

<Auth0Provider
  domain="YOUR_AUTH0_DOMAIN"
  clientId="YOUR_AUTH0_CLIENT_ID"
  redirectUri={window.location.origin}
>
  <App />
</Auth0Provider>
```

2. **Implement Password Change Backend**: The password change functionality currently has a placeholder. You'll need to:
   - Set up a backend API endpoint
   - Use Auth0 Management API to change passwords
   - Or redirect users to Auth0's password change page

3. **Update Authentication Flow**: Replace the current Supabase authentication with Auth0 throughout the app

The Profile page now shows the user's email from Auth0 and has a proper password change interface. The logout functionality is already working correctly and will redirect to smartquot.net as requested.

```typescriptreact
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { useAuth0 } from '@auth0/auth0-react';
import { User, Mail, Phone, MapPin, Building, CreditCard, Lock, Save, Edit, X } from 'lucide-react';

const Profile: React.FC = () => {
  const { user, logout } = useAuth0();
  const [editing, setEditing] = useState(false);
  const [profile, setProfile] = useState({
    companyName: 'Acme Pvt Ltd',
    gstNo: '22AAAAA0000A1Z5',
    address: '123, Main Street, City, State, 123456',
    logo: '',
    subscription: 'Pro Plan (valid till 2025-12-31)',
    phone: '+91-9876543210',
  });
  const [passwords, setPasswords] = useState({ current: '', new: '', confirm: '' });
  const [logoPreview, setLogoPreview] = useState('');
  const [passwordLoading, setPasswordLoading] = useState(false);
  const [passwordMessage, setPasswordMessage] = useState<{ type: 'success' | 'error', text: string } | null>(null);

  const handleInputChange = (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement | HTMLTextAreaElement>) => {
    const { name, value } = e.target;
    setProfile((prev) => ({ ...prev, [name]: value }));
  };

  const handleLogoChange = (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
    const file = e.target.files?.[0];
    if (file) {
      setProfile((prev) => ({ ...prev, logo: file.name }));
      const reader = new FileReader();
      reader.onload = (ev) => setLogoPreview(ev.target?.result as string);
      reader.readAsDataURL(file);
    }
  };

  const handlePasswordChange = (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
    const { name, value } = e.target;
    setPasswords((prev) => ({ ...prev, [name]: value }));
    // Clear any previous messages when user starts typing
    setPasswordMessage(null);
  };

  const handleSave = () => {
    setEditing(false);
    // Save logic here - you can integrate with your backend
  };

  const handlePasswordUpdate = async () => {
    if (passwords.new !== passwords.confirm) {
      setPasswordMessage({ type: 'error', text: 'New passwords do not match' });
      return;
    }

    if (passwords.new.length < 8) {
      setPasswordMessage({ type: 'error', text: 'Password must be at least 8 characters long' });
      return;
    }

    setPasswordLoading(true);
    setPasswordMessage(null);

    try {
      // For Auth0 password change, you would typically:
      // 1. Use Auth0 Management API to change password
      // 2. Or redirect to Auth0's password change page
      // 3. Or use Auth0's password reset flow
      
      // For now, we'll simulate the process
      // In a real implementation, you would call your backend API
      // which would use Auth0 Management API to update the password
      
      await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 1000)); // Simulate API call
      
      setPasswordMessage({ type: 'success', text: 'Password updated successfully!' });
      setPasswords({ current: '', new: '', confirm: '' });
      
      // Optionally, you can redirect to Auth0's password change page:
      // window.location.href = `https://YOUR_AUTH0_DOMAIN/authorize?response_type=code&client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID&redirect_uri=${encodeURIComponent(window.location.origin)}&scope=openid%20profile%20email&audience=YOUR_API_IDENTIFIER&prompt=login`;
      
    } catch (error) {
      setPasswordMessage({ type: 'error', text: 'Failed to update password. Please try again.' });
    } finally {
      setPasswordLoading(false);
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="max-w-4xl mx-auto p-6">
      <div className="bg-white rounded-lg shadow-md">
        {/* Header */}
        <div className="border-b border-gray-200 px-6 py-4">
          <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-800">Profile Settings</h1>
          <p className="text-gray-600 mt-1">Manage your account information and preferences</p>
        </div>

        <div className="p-6">
          {/* Profile Picture and Basic Info */}
          <div className="flex flex-col items-center mb-8">
            <div className="mb-4">
              {logoPreview || profile.logo ? (
                <img
                  src={logoPreview || profile.logo}
                  alt="Company Logo"
                  className="h-24 w-24 object-cover rounded-full border border-gray-300"
                />
              ) : (
                <div className="h-24 w-24 flex items-center justify-center bg-gradient-to-br from-blue-100 to-purple-100 rounded-full border border-gray-300">
                  <User className="w-12 h-12 text-gray-400" />
                </div>
              )}
            </div>
            {editing && (
              <div className="flex items-center space-x-2">
                <input 
                  type="file" 
                  accept="image/*" 
                  onChange={handleLogoChange}
                  className="text-sm text-gray-600 file:mr-4 file:py-2 file:px-4 file:rounded-full file:border-0 file:text-sm file:font-semibold file:bg-blue-50 file:text-blue-700 hover:file:bg-blue-100"
                />
              </div>
            )}
          </div>

          {/* Profile Information */}
          <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-6 mb-8">
            <div>
              <label className="block text-gray-700 font-medium mb-2 flex items-center">
                <Building className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
                Company Name
              </label>
              {editing ? (
                <input
                  type="text"
                  name="companyName"
                  value={profile.companyName}
                  onChange={handleInputChange}
                  className="w-full border border-gray-300 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-transparent"
                />
              ) : (
                <div className="text-gray-900 bg-gray-50 px-3 py-2 rounded-lg">{profile.companyName}</div>
              )}
            </div>

            <div>
              <label className="block text-gray-700 font-medium mb-2">GST Number</label>
              {editing ? (
                <input
                  type="text"
                  name="gstNo"
                  value={profile.gstNo}
                  onChange={handleInputChange}
                  className="w-full border border-gray-300 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-transparent"
                />
              ) : (
                <div className="text-gray-900 bg-gray-50 px-3 py-2 rounded-lg">{profile.gstNo}</div>
              )}
            </div>

            <div>
              <label className="block text-gray-700 font-medium mb-2 flex items-center">
                <Mail className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
                Email Address
              </label>
              <div className="text-gray-900 bg-gray-50 px-3 py-2 rounded-lg flex items-center">
                {user?.email || 'user@example.com'}
                <span className="ml-2 text-xs bg-blue-100 text-blue-800 px-2 py-1 rounded-full">From Auth0</span>
              </div>
            </div>

            <div>
              <label className="block text-gray-700 font-medium mb-2 flex items-center">
                <Phone className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
                Phone Number
              </label>
              {editing ? (
                <input
                  type="text"
                  name="phone"
                  value={profile.phone}
                  onChange={handleInputChange}
                  className="w-full border border-gray-300 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-transparent"
                />
              ) : (
                <div className="text-gray-900 bg-gray-50 px-3 py-2 rounded-lg">{profile.phone}</div>
              )}
            </div>

            <div className="md:col-span-2">
              <label className="block text-gray-700 font-medium mb-2 flex items-center">
                <MapPin className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
                Address
              </label>
              {editing ? (
                <textarea
                  name="address"
                  value={profile.address}
                  onChange={handleInputChange}
                  rows={3}
                  className="w-full border border-gray-300 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-transparent"
                />
              ) : (
                <div className="text-gray-900 bg-gray-50 px-3 py-2 rounded-lg">{profile.address}</div>
              )}
            </div>

            <div className="md:col-span-2">
              <label className="block text-gray-700 font-medium mb-2 flex items-center">
                <CreditCard className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
                Subscription Details
              </label>
              <div className="text-gray-900 bg-gray-50 px-3 py-2 rounded-lg">{profile.subscription}</div>
            </div>
          </div>

          {/* Action Buttons */}
          <div className="flex justify-end space-x-3">
            {editing ? (
              <>
                <button
                  className="px-4 py-2 bg-gray-200 text-gray-700 rounded-lg hover:bg-gray-300 transition-colors flex items-center"
                  onClick={() => setEditing(false)}
                >
                  <X className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
                  Cancel
                </button>
                <button
                  className="px-4 py-2 bg-blue-600 text-white rounded-lg hover:bg-blue-700 transition-colors flex items-center"
                  onClick={handleSave}
                >
                  <Save className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
                  Save Changes
                </button>
              </>
            ) : (
              <button
                className="px-4 py-2 bg-blue-600 text-white rounded-lg hover:bg-blue-700 transition-colors flex items-center"
                onClick={() => setEditing(true)}
              >
                <Edit className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
                Edit Profile
              </button>
            )}
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>

      {/* Change Password Section */}
      <div className="bg-white rounded-lg shadow-md mt-6">
        <div className="border-b border-gray-200 px-6 py-4">
          <h2 className="text-lg font-semibold text-gray-800 flex items-center">
            <Lock className="w-5 h-5 mr-2" />
            Change Password
          </h2>
          <p className="text-gray-600 mt-1">Update your password for enhanced security</p>
        </div>
        
        <div className="p-6">
          <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-4 mb-4">
            <div>
              <label className="block text-gray-700 font-medium mb-2">Current Password</label>
              <input
                type="password"
                name="current"
                value={passwords.current}
                onChange={handlePasswordChange}
                placeholder="Enter current password"
                className="w-full border border-gray-300 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-transparent"
              />
            </div>
            <div>
              <label className="block text-gray-700 font-medium mb-2">New Password</label>
              <input
                type="password"
                name="new"
                value={passwords.new}
                onChange={handlePasswordChange}
                placeholder="Enter new password"
                className="w-full border border-gray-300 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-transparent"
              />
            </div>
          </div>
          
          <div className="mb-4">
            <label className="block text-gray-700 font-medium mb-2">Confirm New Password</label>
            <input
              type="password"
              name="confirm"
              value={passwords.confirm}
              onChange={handlePasswordChange}
              placeholder="Confirm new password"
              className="w-full border border-gray-300 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-transparent"
            />
          </div>

          {passwordMessage && (
            <div className={`mb-4 p-3 rounded-lg ${
              passwordMessage.type === 'success' 
                ? 'bg-green-50 border border-green-200 text-green-700' 
                : 'bg-red-50 border border-red-200 text-red-700'
            }`}>
              {passwordMessage.text}
            </div>
          )}

          <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
            <p className="text-sm text-gray-600">
              Password must be at least 8 characters long
            </p>
            <button 
              className="px-6 py-2 bg-green-600 text-white rounded-lg hover:bg-green-700 transition-colors disabled:opacity-50 disabled:cursor-not-allowed flex items-center"
              onClick={handlePasswordUpdate}
              disabled={passwordLoading || !passwords.current || !passwords.new || !passwords.confirm}
            >
              {passwordLoading ? (
                <div className="w-4 h-4 border-2 border-white border-t-transparent rounded-full animate-spin mr-2" />
              ) : (
                <Lock className="w-4 h-4 mr-2" />
              )}
              Update Password
            </button>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Profile;
```

## Option 2: Full Auth0 Integration (If you want to switch to Auth0)

3. **Configure Auth0 settings** in your Auth0 dashboard
